Items.Title: 作为一名大学生，如何建立一个令人印象深刻的 Python 开发者简历？ | HOW TO BUILD AN IMPRESSIVE PYTHON DEVELOPER RESUME AS A COLLEGE STUDENT?
Items.Description:
由于 Python 恰好是增长最快的编程语言，Python 开发人员的需求量很大

由于其庞大的库和灵活的结构，它易于学习，具有一致且易于解析的语法，可用于人工智能应用。 在当今数据驱动的市场中，Python 开发人员的需求量很大。 根据最新报告（截至 2019 年），由于 Python 的高度灵活性、适应性和多功能性，全球 42% 的开发人员使用 Python。 由于 Python 恰好是增长最快的编程语言之一，Python 中的工作数量自然而然地呈上升趋势。 无论是入门级的 Python 工作还是需要经验的角色，Python 角色在高级和初级级别都同样有利可图。 本文将指导您创建令人印象深刻的 Python 开发人员简历。

首先，您必须了解作为 Python 开发人员将承担的角色和职责。

  • 编写有效且可扩展的 Python 代码
  • 设计和实现健壮的应用程序
  • 调试应用程序以确保低延迟和高可用性
  • 将面向用户的元素与服务器端逻辑集成
  • 实施安全和数据保护
  • 适应各种数据存储解决方案

您要了解的第二件事是成为 Python 开发人员的关键要求

  • 计算机科学、工程或相关领域的学位
  • 你有 Python 开发者的经验
  • 熟悉 Django、Flask 或类似的 Python 框架
  • 熟悉前端技术，如 JavaScript、HTML5 和 CSS3
  • 熟悉各种测试工具。

创建令人印象深刻的简历的步骤
选择格式
您的简历布局是您可以用来突出您的背景和能力的格式、顺序和风格。 您选择的布局可能取决于您必须展示多少经验。 如果您有几年的经验，请使用按时间顺序排列的格式。 功能布局更关注您的技能，使其成为入门级候选人的有效格式。 如果您拥有相当数量的经验和技能，请考虑使用组合布局来突出显示这两个证书。

添加您的联系信息
在文档的左上角列出您的姓名和其他联系信息。 在第一行包括您的名字和姓氏。 以下行可以是您的城市和州、电话号码和电子邮件地址。 为了让招聘经理更容易找到你的名字，可以考虑加粗并增加你名字的字体大小。

做总结陈述——经验、技能等。
您的摘要陈述描述了您的工作经验、技能和专业知识，以及您计划如何将这些应用于该职位。 这个陈述通常是一到两句话长。 试着关注你的背景和能力如何为职位和公司增加价值。 您还可以分享任何职业目标，并提及您计划如何在该职位上成长和提高。

描述你的工作经历
突出您的工作职责和背景，以帮助招聘经理了解您在以前的角色中取得的成就。 列出您工作的公司的名称、位置以及您担任该职位的日期。 在此信息下，使用三到五个要点列出您的职责。 以动作动词开头，并尽可能使用数字，以使您提供给公司的结果更加具体和准确。

具有硬、软或技术技能
在单独的技能部分中，包括任何使您非常适合该职位的硬、软或技术技能。 硬技能是您通过工作经验、教育或培训获得的能力。 软技能是你拥有的天赋，你可以在你的角色中进一步发展。 您可以通过学习不同的软件系统和参加认证课程来培养技术技能。 查看职位描述以查找任何关键字，这些关键字是在要求或偏好部分中使用的短语或单词，用于描述招聘经理在理想候选人中寻找的内容。

添加您的教育
在本节中介绍您的教育经历。 包括您就读的学术机构、您获得的学位名称以及您上学的日期。 考虑增加你的百分比或 GPA。 许多雇主要求 Python 开发人员候选人至少获得工程、计算机科学或相关领域的学士学位。

提及您获得的任何认证
使您的简历从其他候选人中脱颖而出的有效方法是包括技术认证。 这些可以帮助您展示您对提高技能和技术知识的奉献精神。 包括您获得的任何编码或软件开发认证，这些认证有助于提高您在职业生涯中的表现。



原文:

As Python happens to be the fastest-growing programming language, Python developers are in high demand

Python is one of the most widely used programming languages in the world, and for good reason. Because of its vast libraries and flexible structure, it’s simple to learn, has consistent and easy-to-parse syntax, and is utilized for artificial intelligence applications. A Python developer is in high demand in today’s data-driven market. As per the most recent reports (until 2019) 42% of developers around the world use Python due to its high flexibility, adaptability, and versatility. As Python happens to be one of the fastest-growing programming languages, the number of jobs in Python is on the rise naturally. Whether it is an entry-level python job or a role that demands experience, Python roles are equally lucrative at both senior and junior levels. This article will guide you in creating an impressive Python developer resume.

First of all, you must know about the roles and responsibilities that you will be having as a Python developer.

  • Writing effective and scalable Python codes
  • Designing and implementing robust applications
  • Debugging applications to ensure low latency and high-availability
  • Integrating user-facing elements with server-side logic
  • Implementing security and data protection
  • Accommodating various data storage solutions

The second important thing for you to know is the key requirements for becoming a Python developer

  • Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
  • You have prior experience as a Python Developer
  • Good knowledge of Django, Flask, or similar Python frameworks
  • Familiarity with front-end technologies, such as JavaScript, HTML5, and CSS3
  • Knowledge of ORM (Object Relational Mapper)
  • Familiarity with various testing tools.

Steps to Create an Impressive Resume
Select a Format
Your resume layout is the format, order, and style that you can use to highlight your background and abilities. The layout you select can depend on how much experience you have to showcase. Use a chronological format if you have several years of experience to feature. A functional layout focuses more closely on your skills, making it an effective format for entry-level candidates. If you have a fairly even amount of experience and skills, consider using a combination layout to highlight both of these credentials.

Add Your Contact Information
List your name and the rest of your contact information in the top left corner of the document. Include your first and last name on the first line. The following lines can be your city and state, phone number, and email address. To make your name easy for hiring managers to locate, consider bolding and increasing the font size of your name.

Make the Summary Statement- Experience, Skills, etc.
Your summary statement describes your work experience, skills, and expertise and how you plan to apply these to the role. This statement is typically one to two sentences long. Try to focus on how your background and abilities can add value to the position and company. You can also share any career goals and mention how you plan to grow and improve in the role.

Describe your Work Experience
Feature your job duties and background to help to hire managers understand what you accomplished in your previous roles. List the name of the company you worked for, its location, and the dates you served in the role. Under this information, list your responsibilities using three to five bullet points. Start each one with an action verb and use numbers, when possible, to make the results you provided the company more specific and accurate.

Feature Hard, Soft, or Technical Skills
In a separate skills section, include any of the hard, soft, or technical skills that make you a strong fit for the position. Hard skills are capabilities you learned through work experience, education, or training. Soft skills are natural talents you have that you can develop further in your role. You can develop technical skills through learning different software systems and taking certification courses. Review the job description to find any keywords, which are phrases or words used in the requirements or preferences sections to describe what hiring managers look for in ideal candidates.

Add your education
Feature your educational experience in this section. Include the academic institution you attended, the title of the degree you earned, and the dates you went to school. Consider adding your percentage or GPA. Many employers require Python developer candidates to earn at least a bachelor’s degree in engineering, computer science, or a related field.

Mention Any Certifications You’ve Earned
An effective way to make your resume stand out from other candidates is to include technical certifications. These can help you demonstrate your dedication to improving your skills and technical knowledge. Include any coding or software development certifications you’ve earned that help improve your performance in your career.

Items.Title: 如何使用基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC) 向第三方提供对您的 AWS 账户的受限和安全访问 | How to provide restricted & secure access to a third party, to your AWS accounts, using Attribute Based Access Control (ABAC)
Items.Description:
许多组织与不同的供应商或第三方合作以提供或使用服务，或者您可能已将您的工作完全外包给供应商。 通常，组织需要向第三方提供对其资源（如服务器、存储、数据库等）的访问权限，但随之而来的问题是“如何为他们提供安全访问权限？ 当您的资源在云上时，这个问题变得更加重要。

这篇博文将展示一个安全的解决方案来回答上述问题。 在本博客的范围内，基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC)、IAM 角色和切换角色功能已用于派生解决方案。 这是解决方案的样子 -

什么是基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC)，它与传统的基于角色的访问控制 (RBAC) 有何不同？

根据AWS 文档- “基于属性的访问控制 (ABAC) 是一种授权策略，它基于属性定义权限。 在 AWS 中，这些属性称为标签。 您可以将标签附加到 IAM 资源，包括 IAM 实体（用户或角色）和 AWS 资源。 您可以为您的 IAM 委托人创建一个 ABAC 策略或一小组策略。 这些 ABAC 策略可以设计为在主体标签与资源标签匹配时允许操作。 而 RBAC 根据一个人的工作职能定义权限，在 AWS 之外称为角色。 在 AWS 中，角色通常是指 IAM 角色，这是您可以承担的 IAM 中的身份。 在 IAM 中，您通过为不同的工作职能创建不同的策略来实施 RBAC。 然后，您将策略附加到身份（IAM 用户、用户组或 IAM 角色）。 作为最佳实践，您授予工作职能所需的最低权限。 这称为授予最小权限。 通过列出工作职能可以访问的特定资源来做到这一点。 使用传统 RBAC 模型的缺点是，当员工添加新资源时，您必须更新策略以允许访问这些资源。

这是实现此功能所需要的：

  1. 您要授予访问权限的第三方的 AWS 账户。 最好有一个专门的 OU 供第三方使用。
  2. 您要授予访问权限的 AWS 账户。 需要与第三方 IAM 角色关联的 IAM 策略

以下是我为此演示创建的内容。

第 1 步：AWS 组织中的 OU 结构
一个名为“OU-3rd-party”的 OU 及其下的 AWS 账户用于第三方，另一个名为“OU-development”的 OU 和一个 AWS 账户用于属于想要构建其工作负载的组织的开发。

这两个都是 root 帐户，因此必须限制任何人访问。

请注意，第 3 方 OU 上没有服务控制策略 (SCP)。 我稍后会解释为什么

步骤 2：为第三方创建 IAM 用户
为第 3 方创建一个 IAM 用户，以便它可以从开发（或任何其他）AWS 账户中代入 IAM 角色，但前提是用户和角色标签匹配。 以下策略允许用户在您的账户中使用3rdPartyAccessRole名称前缀担任任何角色。 该角色还必须使用与用户相同的团队标签进行标记。

现在，登录到 Development AWS 账户，在我的例子中是836016191915 。 将创建需要由第三方用户承担的 IAM 角色。

第 3 步：在开发账户中创建由第三方用户承担的 ABAC 策略和 IAM 角色
以下称为3rdparty-access-policy允许委托人读取机密、启动和停止 EC2，但前提是这些资源被标记为与委托人相同的键值对。

 { "Version": "2012-10-17", "Statement": [ { "Sid": "secretsmanagerreadonlyforsameteam", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "secretsmanager:Describe*", "secretsmanager:List*", "secretsmanager:Get*" ], "Resource": "*", "Condition": { "StringEquals": { "aws:ResourceTag/access-team": "${aws:PrincipalTag/access-team}" } } }, { "Sid": "secretmanagerlistsecretsforAll", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "secretsmanager:GetRandomPassword", "secretsmanager:ListSecrets" ], "Resource": "*" }, { "Sid": "allowEC2startstopforsameteam", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": [ "ec2:StartInstances", "ec2:StopInstances" ], "Resource": "arn:aws:ec2:*:*:instance/*", "Condition": { "StringEquals": { "aws:ResourceTag/access-team": "${aws:PrincipalTag/access-team}" } } }, { "Sid": "describeEC2forAll", "Effect": "Allow", "Action": "ec2:DescribeInstances", "Resource": "*" } ] }

在 IAM 角色之后创建3rdPartyAccessRole ，并将在上一步中创建的3rdparty-access-policy附加到它。 该角色还需要使用与资源和主体标签相同的标签进行标记。

现在都完成了！ 现在是测试的时候了。

使用创建的第三方 IAM 用户登录 AWS 控制台。 就我而言，它是用户3rd-party-vendor-user 。 登录后，它无法访问其 AWS 账户中的任何资源。

现在让第三方用户通过切换角色功能从开发账户836016191915承担角色3rdPartyAccessRole或者可以与他们共享 IAM 角色中生成的切换角色链接 -

我将使用切换角色选项：

输入开发帐号、创建的 IAM 角色和显示名称（可选），然后单击 Switch Role 按钮：

登录后，单击帐户信息下拉菜单以查看更多详细信息。 请注意“当前活动为”和“帐户 ID”字段，它是 IAM 角色和 AWS 帐户 ID，即假定的第 3 方用户。 要验证谁担任了该角色，请参阅下面同一面板上的“签名为”字段。

现在，让我们测试一下权限。 在开发帐户中，第三方用户只能列出和获取与用户标签相同的秘密的值，即access-team = 3rdpartyuser 。 该策略还允许第三方用户仅启动和停止那些标记为与用户标签相同的 EC2。

好的。 在 Secrets manager 服务中，存储了两个秘密：第一，没有标签access-team = 3rdpartyuser的“演示秘密”，这意味着第三方用户无法检索密码，而第二个秘密， secret-3rdparty可供第三方用户访问。 请参阅下面的设置：

第三方用户，根据权限，可以列出秘密

尝试访问 demo-secret 时（请记住，它与 IAM 用户没有相同的标签），它会收到一个错误：

现在，当访问第二个秘密secret-3rdparty时，由于资源标签与主体标签匹配，它能够成功检索密码：

伟大的！ 让我们也测试一下 EC2 权限。 在开发帐户中，有一个 EC2 处于停止状态并标记为access-team = aws-community-builder ，这意味着登录的第三方用户应该无法启动此 EC2。

让我们看看，正如预期的那样，它无法启动实例：

他们可以验证为什么他们无法访问，因为资源不属于他们：

做得好！ 现在，为什么没有拒绝 SCP 购买仍然第三方用户无权访问任何 AWS 资源的答案是因为默认情况下拒绝访问 IAM 委托人，必须明确允许他们执行操作。 否则，它们将被隐式拒绝访问。

此处，第三方 IAM 用户在基于身份的策略中明确允许，仅对于sts:AssumeRole ，其余权限被隐式拒绝。 以下是策略评估逻辑的工作原理：

如果您的使用案例需要使用 AWS CLI 或 AWS API 来承担该角色，那么您可以使用外部 ID 引入更多安全性；请参阅文档 -https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide /id_roles_create_for-user_externalid.html

如果您的公司使用基于 SAML 的身份提供商 (IdP) 来管理企业用户身份，您可以使用 SAML 属性在 AWS 中进行细粒度的访问控制。 属性可以包括成本中心标识符、用户电子邮件地址、部门分类和项目分配。 当您将这些属性作为会话标签传递时，您可以根据这些会话标签控制对 AWS 的访问。 遵循 AWS 文档IAM 教程：为 ABAC 使用 SAML 会话标签。

在这篇博文中，我展示了如何使用基于属性的访问控制，通过 AWS 控制台向需要访问这些 AWS 资源的第三方提供对 AWS 账户中 AWS 资源的安全访问。

我希望你从这篇博文中学到了一些东西。 如果您喜欢阅读这篇博文，请鼓掌并关注我以获得更多有趣的帖子。

到那时快乐学习！

好样的！ 要开心！ :) :)



原文:

Many Organizations work with different vendors or third parties, to provide or consume services or you may have outsourced your work completely to the vendor. Often there is a need for Organizations to provide access to their resources like server, storage, database etc. to third parties but then the questions comes “HOW to give them secure access?. The question becomes more important when your resources are on the cloud.

This blog post will showcase a secure solution to answer above question. There are services and features in AWS which can be leveraged to achieve the above goal. Services like IAM, Attribute Based Access Control (ABAC) are much powerful to help in this regard. IAM provides permissions and policies, allows switching between IAM roles, and other features.

For the scope of this blog, Attribute Based Access Control (ABAC), IAM Role & Switch role functionalities have been used to derive the solution. Here is how the solution looks like-

What is Attribute Based Access Control (ABAC) and how it is different from traditional Role Based Access Control (RBAC)?

As per AWS documentation - “Attribute-based access control (ABAC) is an authorization strategy that defines permissions based on attributes. In AWS, these attributes are called tags. You can attach tags to IAM resources, including IAM entities (users or roles) and to AWS resources. You can create a single ABAC policy or small set of policies for your IAM principals. These ABAC policies can be designed to allow operations when the principal’s tag matches the resource tag. ABAC is helpful in environments that are growing rapidly and helps with situations where policy management becomes cumbersome.”

While RBAC defines permissions based on a person’s job function, known outside of AWS as a role. Within AWS a role usually refers to an IAM role, which is an identity in IAM that you can assume. IAM does include managed policies for job functions that align permissions to a job function in an RBAC model. In IAM, you implement RBAC by creating different policies for different job functions. You then attach the policies to identities (IAM users, groups of users, or IAM roles). As a best practice, you grant the minimum permissions necessary for the job function. This is known as granting least privilege. Do this by listing the specific resources that the job function can access. The disadvantage to using the traditional RBAC model is that when employees add new resources, you must update policies to allow access to those resources.

Here is what you need for this to get implemented:

  1. an AWS account for the third party whom you want to give access to. Better to have a dedicated OU for the third party.
  2. AWS account(s) which you want to give access of. OU strategy may differ per organization
  3. IAM policies which need to be associated with IAM role for third parties

Below is what I have created for the purpose of this demo.

Step 1: OU structure in AWS Organization
An OU called ‘OU-3rd-party’ and an AWS Account underneath it, for the third party and another OU called ‘OU-development’ and an AWS account underneath for the development which belongs to the organization want to build their workload on.

Both these are root accounts, hence MUST be restricted from giving access to anybody.

Note there is no Service Control Policy (SCP) on the 3rd-party OU. I’ll explain later why

Step 2: Create IAM users for third party
Create an IAM user for the 3rd party, so that it can assume the IAM role from the development (or any other) AWS account(s) but only when the user and role tags match. The following policy allows a user to assume any role in your account with the 3rdPartyAccessRole name prefix. The role must also be tagged with the same team tag as the user.

Now, login to the Development AWS account, in my case it is 836016191915. An IAM role which needs to be assumed by the third party user is to be created.

Step 3: Create ABAC Policy & IAM role to be assumed by the third party user, in the development account
The following policy called 3rdparty-access-policy allows principals to read secrets, start and stop EC2s but only when those resources are tagged with the same key-value pairs as the principal.

{
    "Version": "2012-10-17",
    "Statement": [
        {
            "Sid": "secretsmanagerreadonlyforsameteam",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "secretsmanager:Describe*",
                "secretsmanager:List*",
                "secretsmanager:Get*"
            ],
            "Resource": "*",
            "Condition": {
                "StringEquals": {
                    "aws:ResourceTag/access-team": "${aws:PrincipalTag/access-team}"
                }
            }
        },
        {
            "Sid": "secretmanagerlistsecretsforAll",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "secretsmanager:GetRandomPassword",
                "secretsmanager:ListSecrets"
            ],
            "Resource": "*"
        },
        {
            "Sid": "allowEC2startstopforsameteam",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": [
                "ec2:StartInstances",
                "ec2:StopInstances"
            ],
            "Resource": "arn:aws:ec2:*:*:instance/*",
            "Condition": {
                "StringEquals": {
                    "aws:ResourceTag/access-team": "${aws:PrincipalTag/access-team}"
                }
            }
        },
        {
            "Sid": "describeEC2forAll",
            "Effect": "Allow",
            "Action": "ec2:DescribeInstances",
            "Resource": "*"
        }
    ]
}

Following IAM role 3rdPartyAccessRole is created and the 3rdparty-access-policy that was created in the previous step is attached to it. The role also needs to be tagged with the same tag as the resource and the principal tag.

All done now! It is the time to test.

Login to the AWS Console, with the third party IAM user created. In my case, it is the user 3rd-party-vendor-user. Upon login, it doesn’t have access to any of the resources in its AWS account.

Now lets ask the third party user to assume the role 3rdPartyAccessRole from the development account 836016191915via Switch role functionality OR the switch role link generated in the IAM role can be shared with them -

I’ll use the Switch role option:

Enter the development account number, the IAM role created and the display name (optional) and click on Switch Role button:

Upon login, click the account information drop down to see additional details. Notice the ‘currently active as’ & ‘Account ID’ fields, it is the IAM role & the AWS Account ID, the 3rd party user assumed. To verify who has assumed the role, see the ‘Signed is as’ field below on the same panel.

Now, lets test the permissions. While in the development account, third party user is allowed to list and get the value of only those secrets which are tagged as same as the user’s tag i.e. access-team = 3rdpartyuser. The policy also allows the third party user to start and stop only those EC2s which are tagged as same as the user’s tag.

OK. In the Secrets manager service, there are two secrets stored: First, the ‘demo-secret’ which doesn’t have the tag access-team = 3rdpartyuser which means the third party user can’t retrieve the password whereas the second secret, secret-3rdparty is accessible to the third party user. See the setup below:

Third party user, as per the permissions, can list the secrets

When trying to access the demo-secret (remember it doesn’t have the same tag as the IAM user) it gets an error:

Now, when accessing the second secret secret-3rdparty, it is able to retrieve the password successfully since the resource tag matches the principal tag:

Great! Lets test the EC2 permissions as well. In the Development account, there is one EC2 in stopped state and tagged as access-team = aws-community-builder which means logged in third party user should not be able to start this EC2.

Lets see and as expected, it can not Start the instance:

They can verify why they can not access as the resource doesn’t belong to them:

Well done! :)

Now, the answer why there is no Deny SCP buy still the third party user doesn’t have permissions to any AWS resources, is because an IAM principal is denied access by default, they must be explicitly allowed to perform an action. Otherwise, they are implicitly denied access.

Here, the third party IAM user has explicit Allow in the Identity based policy, for sts:AssumeRole only, rest of the permissions are implicitly denied. Here is how the policy evaluation logic works:

*source: AWS documentation Policy evaluation logic

If your use case requires the use of AWS CLI or AWS API to assume that role then you can introduce more security by using the External ID;refer the documentation - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-user_externalid.html

If your company uses a SAML-based identity provider (IdP) to manage corporate user identities, you can use SAML attributes for fine-grained access control in AWS. Attributes can include cost center identifiers, user email addresses, department classifications, and project assignments. When you pass these attributes as session tags, you can then control access to AWS based on these session tags. Follow the AWS documentation IAM tutorial: Use SAML session tags for ABAC.

With this blog post, I have showcased how you can provide secure access to the AWS resources in your AWS accounts, to the Third parties which require access to those AWS resources, through AWS Console, using attribute based access control.

I hope you have learned something from this blog post. If you liked reading this blog post, please clap and follow me for more interesting posts.

Till then Happy Learning!

Be AWSome! Be Happy! :)
Items.Author: somesh1983

Items.Title: 7个你必须知道的Windows命令提示符 | 7 Command Prompts for Windows you must know
Items.Description:
它继续存在并且是 Windows 上一个被低估的工具：命令提示符。
您应该知道很多命令，因为它们对您的日常活动很有用，并且比许多 UI 界面更快。

我选择了七个最有用的命令提示符。

此命令显示您计算机的 IP 地址。
如果你想更新你的 IP 地址，你可以启动命令ipconfig /release然后ipconfig /renew 。
使用参数 /flushdns 您将刷新您的 DNS 地址。

网络统计

此命令显示活动的开放端口和相关的 IP 地址。

命令 ping 向指定的地址或 IP 地址发送四个测试数据包（默认为四个）。
如果您能够与远程地址通信，您会看到往返时间。

示踪剂

此命令与 Pathping 非常相似，但它显示每个跃点需要执行多少时间。

关闭

我认为这个命令不需要任何介绍。
默认情况下，它会关闭您的计算机，但如果您添加参数 /r，它会重新启动您的计算机。

任务列表 && 任务杀

此命令提供您 PC 上运行的当前进程的列表。

如果要终止正在运行的任务，可以使用命令 taskkill -pid 后跟进程 ID，您可以在前面的命令中找到该进程 ID。

分类

清除命令提示符屏幕。 如果您想在执行大量命令后从头开始重新启动，这很有用。

结论

您可以从命令提示符中使用更多命令。 这些只是我每天经常使用的最常见的。

在命令中写下你最喜欢的命令，我会更新帖子。 😉 😉



原文:

It continue to exist and is a very underrated tool on Windows: Command Prompt.
There are a lot of commands that you should know because they are useful for your daily activities and are more quick than a lot of UI interfaces.

I choose seven of the most useful command prompts.

Ipconfig

This command shows the IP Address of your computer.
If you want to renew your IP Address, you can launch the command ipconfig /release and then ipconfig /renew .
With the parameter /flushdns you are goingo to refresh your DNS address.

Netstat

This command shows the active open ports and the related IP Address.

Ping & PathPing

The command ping sends four test packets (four by default) to the specified address or IP Address.
If you are able to communicate with the remote address, you see the time of the roundtrip.

The pathping command is very similar but it shows all the information about the route of the packages.

Tracert

This command in very similar to the Pathping but it shows how much time each hops needs to be performed.

Shutdown

I think this command doesn't need any introduction.
By default it shutdowns your computer, but if you add the parameter /r, it restarts your computer.

Tasklist && Taskkill

This command provides a list of the current processes running on your PC.

If you want to kill a running task, you can use the command taskkill -pid followed by the process ID that you can find the in previous command.

Cls

Clear the command prompt screen. It useful if you want to restart from scratch after a lot of commands.

Conclusion

There are much more commands that you can use from the Command Prompt. These are just the most common which I use everyday a lot of time.

Write your favorite command in the commands and I will update the post. 😉
Items.Author: kasuken

Items.Title: 您可以尝试的 7 个最佳 Web 开发理念 | 7 Best Ideas for Web Development that You can Try
Items.Description:
古普瑞特·辛格

发表于毫无疑问，每个人都明白掌握 Web 开发（或任何其他技术技能）的最佳方法就是实践它！ 然而，大多数人，尤其是新手，都会犯错误，只专注于研究原理，而推迟启动项目太久。 是的，让您的概念尽可能清晰以掌握 Web 开发能力很重要； 但是如果没有必要的实践经验，你不能期望成为一名熟练的 Web 开发人员。 新手在掌握 Web 开发的道路上犯的另一个常见错误是试图立即开发复杂和高级的项目。 并最终导致各种不良影响，如缺乏兴趣和动力、前后不一等。 与其直接跳入建立像 Flipkart 或 Facebook 这样的成熟网站，初学者应该从基本项目开始，以获得基本概念和技术的实践经验，然后再学习中级和高级知识。 在本文中，我们将为您提供各种有用的 Web 开发项目想法列表，这些想法面向初学者，无疑将帮助您评估您的基本 Web 开发能力——主要是 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript。 让我们详细阐述 Web 开发的最佳想法！

作为您的第一个 Web 开发项目，您可能想尝试设计一个引人入胜的登录页面。 但是，什么是着陆页？ 好的，登陆页面是一个独立的网页，旨在推广或宣传电子商务行业中的某种产品或服务。

登录页面可以使用 HTML 和 CSS 创建，它需要您设置页面的基本结构，例如插入页眉和页脚、生成列、划分元素、创建导航栏、背景和样式等. 为了使其更加独特和有趣，您需要考虑填充、边距、间距等问题。

答案是肯定的，个人博客可能完成类似的目标。 虽然博客可以从简单到复杂的任何级别制作，但您应该从使用 HTML、CSS 和 jQuery 构建一个基本的 WordPress 网站开始。 如果您对 CSS Grid、Flexbox、Responsive Build 和其他基本概念有很好的掌握，那么您将更容易设计一个有趣的个人博客项目。 同时，Bootstrap 允许您快速轻松地创建可在各种屏幕尺寸上运行的可自定义的博客。

您可以在当今数字驱动的世界中构建一个基本的静态业务组合网站作为初学者级项目，而实际上每个企业都在上线。 它可能基于任何类型的公司，例如餐厅、时尚服装、运动器材等，您只需展示商家提供的物品和服务，以及相关照片、价格、联系方式信息等。 要进行多列布局，您将协调这些产品照片中的每一张并使用网格方法。

一个包含基本算术运算（如加法、减法、乘法和除法）的计算器项目对于了解 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 的新手来说将非常有益。 您需要创建一个带有数据输入按钮和数字显示器的应用程序来显示结果。 一旦您在 Web 开发工具和技术方面获得了更多经验，您就可以考虑为这个计算器项目添加复杂的功能。

待办事项列表应用程序将具有动态和响应式 UI，允许您为日常琐事创建多个待办事项列表，并轻松添加和删除其中的内容。 您需要对该项目的 HTML、CSS、JavaScript、jQuery 和 Bootstrap 有基本的了解。 此外，了解 Bootstrap 的网格系统是如何工作的也会很有用。 在这个项目中，您将主要使用 JavaScript 方法来执行以下任务：将用户输入添加到列表中、从列表中删除条目、在屏幕上显示项目等等。 在结合了添加和删除项目、制作大量列表等基本功能之后。

对于那些刚接触 Web 编程并想涉足的人来说，开发一个问答游戏也是一个可行的选择。 在这个项目中，您必须开发一个网页，该网页具有多种选择题和四个选项，以及测验的结果，一旦用户提交测试，就会显示该结果。 为了使网站看起来更具吸引力和创意，您需要利用您的 HTML 能力来构建框架和 CSS 人才，以确保页面看起来更有趣和更有创意。 在这个项目中，JavaScript 将负责诸如确定响应是否有效、对正确和错误答案做出适当反应、显示提交结果等职责。

什么人不喜欢一个好的模因？ 它适用于所有人。 但是你知道你可以用一些基本的网络编程技能来构建你自己的模因创建者吗？ 所以，这就是你如何去做。 您需要基本掌握 HTML、CSS 和 JavaScript 来构建这个响应式 meme 生成器。

您可以通过为照片添加有趣或可怕的标题来制作自己的模因。 您可以使用 HTML 创建系统的结构和反馈表单以收集用户的照片和文本行，以及使用 CSS 使其看起来更有吸引力。 然后，使用 JavaScript 代码，您必须更改图像以制作令人惊叹的模因。

最后，您是否设法提出了 2022 年的任何一种 Web 开发概念？ 希望你成功了。 所有上述关于网页设计项目的标准都很容易实现。 但是，无论您使用哪个选项，请确保使用高质量的网站测试清单。

一旦你这样做了，你就可以确定你走在正确的轨道上，而 2022 年很有可能成为你繁荣未来的开端。 最重要的是选择一个考虑到你的能力和兴趣的概念。 没有天赋就没有成功，如果你不喜欢你正在做的事情，你就不会有效。



原文:

Gurpreet Singh

Posted on May 21

Without a doubt, everyone understands that the greatest method to master Web Development (or any other technical skill) is to practice it! However, most people, especially novices, make the mistake of focusing solely on studying the principles and delaying starting a project for far too long. Yes, it is important to make your notions as clear as possible to master web development abilities; but you cannot expect to become a skilled Web Developer without the necessary practical experience. Another common blunder made by novices on their path to mastering Web Development is attempting to develop sophisticated and advanced-level projects right away. And it eventually leads to a variety of undesirable effects, such as a lack of interest and drive, inconsistency, and so on. Rather than jumping right into establishing a full-fledged website like Flipkart or Facebook, beginners should begin with basic projects to gain practical experience with fundamental concepts and technologies before moving forward with intermediate and advanced knowledge. In this article, we'll provide you with a list of various useful web development project ideas, geared toward beginners that will undoubtedly help you evaluate your fundamental web development abilities - mostly HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Let's elaborate the best ideas for web development!

1.Landing Page

As your first Web Development project, you might want to attempt designing an engaging Landing Page. But, what is a landing page? Okay, a landing page is a standalone web page designed to promote or advertise a certain product or service in the e-commerce industry.

A landing page may be created using HTML and CSS, and it will need you to set the page's basic structure, such as inserting a header and footer, producing columns, dividing elements, creating a navigation bar, background, and style, among other things. To make it more unique and interesting, you'll want to think about things like padding, margin, spacing, and so on.

2.Personal Website What if you could create a project that would not only help you improve your web development abilities but also serve as a platform for you to share your knowledge and experiences with others? The answer is yes, a personal blog may finish a similar goal. Though a blog may be made at any level from simple to complex, you should start by building a basic WordPress website using HTML, CSS, and jQuery. If you have a great grasp of CSS Grid, Flexbox, Responsive Build, and other essential concepts, it will be easier for you to design an intriguing Personal Blog project. Meanwhile, Bootstrap allows you to create a customizable weblog that works on a range of screen sizes fast and effortlessly.

3.Website for a Portfolio of Work

You may construct a basic static Business Portfolio Website as a beginner-level project in today's digitally driven world when practically every business is going online. It may be based on any type of company, such as a restaurant, fashion clothing, sports equipment, and so on, and all you have to do is present the items and services given by the business, along with relevant photographs, prices, contact information, and so on. To make a multi-column layout, you'll coordinate every one of these product photos and use the grid method.

4.Calculator

A calculator project that includes fundamental arithmetic operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division will be quite beneficial to a newbie who understands HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. You'll need to create an application with data entry buttons and a digital display to show the findings. The CSS Grid can be used to arrange buttons and a screen in a grid-like manner. You could consider adding complex features to this calculator project once you've gained more experience with web development tools and technologies.

5.App for Making a To-Do List

A To-Do List App will feature a dynamic and responsive UI that allows you to create several to-do lists for everyday chores and easily add and delete things from them. You'll need a basic understanding of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, jQuery, and Bootstrap for this project. Additionally, understanding how Bootstrap's grid system works would be useful. In this project, you'll mostly utilize JavaScript methods to perform the following tasks: adding user input to lists, removing entries from lists, displaying items on the screen, and so on. After incorporating the fundamental features such as adding and deleting items, making numerous lists, and so on.

6.Project for a Quiz Game

Developing a Quiz Game is also a feasible choice for those who are new to web programming and want to get their feet wet. In this project, you must develop a web page that has various multiple-choice alternatives with four options, as well as the quiz's result, which will be presented once the user submits the test. To make the website appear more engaging and creative, you'll need to utilize your HTML abilities to build up the framework and CSS talent to ensure the page looks more interesting and creative. In this project, the JavaScript will be accountable for responsibilities such as determining if the response is valid or not, reacting appropriately for correct and incorrect answers, showing the outcome on submission, and so on.

7.The Meme Generator Projects

What person doesn't like a good meme? It applies to everyone. But did you know that you can construct your own meme creator with some basic web programming skills? So, this is how you go about doing it. You'll need a basic grasp of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to build this responsive meme generator.

You'll be able to make your own memes by adding amusing or horrible captions to the photographs. You may utilise HTML to create the system's structure and feedback forms to gather photographs and text lines from users, as well as CSS to make it look more appealing. Then, using JavaScript code, you must change the image to make an amazing meme.

To wrap up, Did you manage to come up with any one web development concept for 2022? Hopefully, you were successful. All of the aforementioned criteria concerning web design projects are simple to accomplish. However, whichever option you use, make sure to use a quality website testing checklist.

Once you've done so, you can be certain that you're on the right track, and the year 2022 has a good potential of becoming the start of your prosperous future. Items.Title: 对于那些在设置 Datadog RUM 时遇到问题的人 | For those who have trouble setting up Datadog RUM
Items.Description:
辣椒粉

发表于如果有人在设置 Datadog RUM 时遇到问题，我想告诉他们， “JS/NPM 版本的设置代码有一些错误。” 我之前曾发短信向 Datadog 支持反馈过，但尚未修复，所以我写了它。

正如您在屏幕截图中看到的，提供了一个片段作为示例，但不幸的是，有一个错字。

看第3行，大括号{已经用过， datadogRum.init({.
但是}从未在代码段中说明过。

这是收集片段。

 import { datadogRum } from ' @datadog/browser-rum ' ; datadogRum . init ({ applicationId : ' hoge-hoge-hoge ' , clientToken : ' hoge-hoge-hoge ' , site : ' datadoghq.com ' , service : ' temp ' , // Specify a version number to identify the deployed version of your application in Datadog // version: '1.0.0', sampleRate : 100 , replaySampleRate : 100 , trackInteractions : true , defaultPrivacyLevel : ' mask-user-input ' , }); datadogRum . startSessionReplayRecording ();

如果你想禁用会话重播，你还必须小心这个设置页面。 有一个切换，Session Replay Enabled，它似乎允许编辑片段以禁用该功能。

但是，仅此设置似乎还不够...😅您还必须设置replaySampleRate: 0

将 replaySampleRate 设置为 0 以停止收集包含重播、资源和长任务的 RUM 会话重播计划。

这就是为什么这里是收集片段！

 import { datadogRum } from ' @datadog/browser-rum ' ; datadogRum . init ({ applicationId : ' 714191a1-eee6-447d-b2e3-40e049c84c81 ' , clientToken : ' pub34b64dab1e6b8a881b380180c1171d17 ' , site : ' datadoghq.com ' , service : ' temp ' , // Specify a version number to identify the deployed version of your application in Datadog // version: '1.0.0', sampleRate : 100 , replaySampleRate : 0 , trackInteractions : true , );

祝监控愉快！

瑞恩卡特赖特 - Dec 9 '21 21 年 12 月 9 日



原文:

paprika-mah

Posted on May 21

If anyone has trouble setting up Datadog RUM, I would like to tell them, "The setup code for JS/NPM version has some incorrect." I have previously texted Datadog support about the feedback, but it has not been fixed, so I wrote it.

1. Typo in the snippet

As you can see in the screenshot, a snippet is provided as a sample, but unfortunately, a typo has.

Look at line 3, the curly bracket { has been used, datadogRum.init({.
But the } never has been stated in the snippet.

Here is the collect snippet.

import { datadogRum } from '@datadog/browser-rum'; datadogRum.init({ applicationId: 'hoge-hoge-hoge', clientToken: 'hoge-hoge-hoge', site: 'datadoghq.com', service:'temp', // Specify a version number to identify the deployed version of your application in Datadog  // version: '1.0.0', sampleRate: 100, replaySampleRate: 100, trackInteractions: true, defaultPrivacyLevel:'mask-user-input',
}); datadogRum.startSessionReplayRecording();

2. Disabled Session Replay

If you wanna disable session replay, you also have to be careful with this setup page. There is a toggle, Session Replay Enabled, which seems to allow the snippet to be edited to disable the function.

However, only this setting doesn't seem sufficient...😅 You also must set replaySampleRate: 0

References

Set replaySampleRate to 0 to stop collecting the RUM Session Replay plan which includes replays, resources, and long tasks.

That's why here is the collect snippet!

import { datadogRum } from '@datadog/browser-rum'; datadogRum.init({ applicationId: '714191a1-eee6-447d-b2e3-40e049c84c81', clientToken: 'pub34b64dab1e6b8a881b380180c1171d17', site: 'datadoghq.com', service:'temp', // Specify a version number to identify the deployed version of your application in Datadog  // version: '1.0.0', sampleRate: 100, replaySampleRate: 0, trackInteractions: true,
);

Happy Monitoring!

Ryan Cartwright - Dec 9 '21
Items.Author: paprikamah

Items.Title: Azure 计算服务概述 | Overview of Azure compute services
Items.Description:
它提供计算资源，例如磁盘、处理器、内存、网络和操作系统。 这些资源是按需提供的，通常可以在几分钟甚至几秒钟内提供。 您只需为使用的资源付费，而且只要您使用它们就可以。

该服务支持 Linux、Windows Server、SQL Server、Oracle、IBM 和 SAP。 每项服务都根据您的要求提供不同的选项。 一些最突出的服务是：

  • 虚拟机

    虚拟机是物理计算机的软件模拟。 它们包括虚拟处理器、内存、存储和网络资源。 使用远程桌面客户端时，您可以像坐在虚拟机前一样使用和控制虚拟机。

    借助 Azure 虚拟机，您可以在云中创建和使用 VM。 虚拟机提供基础架构即服务 (IaaS)，并且可以以不同的方式使用。 当您需要完全控制操作系统和环境时，VM 是理想的选择。 就像物理计算机一样，您可以自定义 VM 上运行的所有软件。 当您运行自定义软件或自定义托管配置时，此功能很有帮助。

    虚拟机规模集

    虚拟机规模集是一种 Azure 计算资源，可用于部署和管理一组相同的 VM。 在所有 VM 配置相同的情况下，虚拟机规模集旨在支持真正的自动缩放。 无需预先配置 VM。 因此，更容易构建针对大计算、大数据和容器化工作负载的大规模服务。 随着需求的增加，可以添加更多的 VM 实例。 随着需求下降，可以删除 VM 实例。 该过程可以是手动的、自动的或两者的组合。

    容器和 Kubernetes

    容器实例和 Azure Kubernetes 服务是可用于部署和管理容器的 Azure 计算资源。 容器是轻量级的虚拟化应用环境。 它们旨在快速创建、横向扩展和动态停止。 您可以在单个主机上运行容器化应用程序的多个实例。

    应用服务

    借助 Azure 应用服务，您可以快速构建、部署和扩展在任何平台上运行的企业级 Web、移动和 API 应用。 您可以在使用完全托管的平台执行基础架构维护的同时满足严格的性能、可扩展性、安全性和合规性要求。 应用服务是一种平台即服务 (PaaS) 产品。

    职能

    当您只关心运行服务的代码而不关心底层平台或基础设施时，函数是理想的选择。 当您需要执行工作以响应来自另一个 Azure 服务的事件（通常通过 REST 请求）、计时器或消息时，并且当该工作可以在几秒钟或更短的时间内快速完成时，通常会使用它们。

    决定何时使用 Azure 虚拟机

    借助 Azure 虚拟机，您可以在云中创建和使用 VM。 就像物理计算机一样，您可以自定义 VM 上运行的所有软件。 当您需要时，VM 是理想的选择：

    • 完全控制操作系统 (OS)。
    • 运行自定义软件的能力。
    • 使用自定义托管配置。

    您仍然需要配置、更新和维护在 VM 上运行的软件。

    选择预配置的 VM 映像后，您可以在几分钟内创建和配置 VM。 选择映像是您在创建 VM 时做出的最重要的决定之一。 映像是用于创建 VM 的模板。 这些模板已经包含操作系统和其他软件，例如开发工具或网络托管环境。

    何时使用 VM 的示例

    • 在测试和开发期间。 然后，测试和开发人员可以在不再需要虚拟机时轻松删除它们。

    • 在云中运行应用程序时。 在公共云中运行某些应用程序而不是创建传统基础设施来运行它们的能力可以提供可观的经济效益。 例如，应用程序可能需要处理需求波动。 在不需要时关闭 VM 或快速启动它们以满足突然增加的需求意味着您只需为使用的资源付费。

    • 将数据中心扩展到云时。 组织可以通过在 Azure 中创建虚拟网络并将 VM 添加到该虚拟网络来扩展其本地网络的功能。 然后，SharePoint 等应用程序可以在 Azure VM 上运行，而不是在本地运行。 这种安排使部署比在本地环境中更容易或更便宜。

    • 在灾难恢复期间。 与在云中运行某些类型的应用程序并将本地网络扩展到云一样，您可以通过使用基于 IaaS 的灾难恢复方法来显着节省成本。 如果主数据中心出现故障，您可以创建在 Azure 上运行的 VM 来运行您的关键应用程序，然后在主数据中心再次运行时将其关闭。

    使用虚拟机迁移到云

    当您从物理服务器迁移到云（也称为提升和转移）时，VM 也是一个很好的选择。 您可以创建物理服务器的映像并将其托管在虚拟机中，只需很少或没有更改。 就像物理本地服务器一样，您必须维护 VM。 您更新已安装的操作系统及其运行的软件。

    在 Azure 中缩放 VM

    您可以运行单个 VM 来进行测试、开发或次要任务。 或者，您可以将 VM 组合在一起以提供高可用性、可扩展性和冗余。 无论您的正常运行时间要求是什么，Azure 都有几个可以满足这些要求的功能。 这些功能包括：

    • 虚拟机规模集
    • 什么是虚拟机规模集？

      虚拟机规模集让你可以创建和管理一组相同的负载平衡 VM。 想象一下，您正在运行一个网站，该网站使科学家能够上传需要处理的天文学图像。 如果你复制了虚拟机，你通常需要配置一个额外的服务来在网站的多个实例之间路由请求。 虚拟机规模集可以为您完成这项工作。

      规模集允许您在几分钟内集中管理、配置和更新大量 VM，以提供高度可用的应用程序。 借助虚拟机规模集，您可以为计算、大数据和容器工作负载等领域构建大规模服务。

      什么是 Azure 批处理？

      当您准备好运行作业时，Batch 会执行以下操作：

      • 为您启动计算 VM 池。
      • 安装应用程序和暂存数据。
      • 运行具有尽可能多的任务的作业。
      • 识别故障。
      • 重新排队工作。
      • 随着工作的完成缩小池。

      在某些情况下，您可能需要原始计算能力或超级计算机级别的计算能力。 Azure provides these capabilities. Azure 提供了这些功能。



原文:

Azure compute is an on-demand computing service for running cloud-based applications. It provides computing resources such as disks, processors, memory, networking, and operating systems. The resources are available on-demand and can typically be made available in minutes or even seconds. You pay only for the resources you use, and only for as long as you're using them.

Azure supports a wide range of computing solutions for development and testing, running applications, and extending your datacenter. The service supports Linux, Windows Server, SQL Server, Oracle, IBM, and SAP. Azure also has many services that can run virtual machines (VMs). Each service provides different options depending on your requirements. Some of the most prominent services are:

  • Azure Virtual Machines
  • Azure Container Instances
  • Azure App Service
  • Azure Functions (or serverless computing)

Virtual machines

Virtual machines are software emulations of physical computers. They include a virtual processor, memory, storage, and networking resources. VMs host an operating system, and you can install and run software just like a physical computer. When using a remote desktop client, you can use and control the VM as if you were sitting in front of it.

With Azure Virtual Machines, you can create and use VMs in the cloud. Virtual Machines provides infrastructure as a service (IaaS) and can be used in different ways. When you need total control over an operating system and environment, VMs are an ideal choice. Just like a physical computer, you can customize all the software running on the VM. This ability is helpful when you're running custom software or custom hosting configurations.

Virtual machine scale sets

Virtual machine scale sets are an Azure compute resource that you can use to deploy and manage a set of identical VMs. With all VMs configured the same, virtual machine scale sets are designed to support true autoscale. No pre-provisioning of VMs is required. For this reason, it's easier to build large-scale services targeting big compute, big data, and containerized workloads. As demand goes up, more VM instances can be added. As demand goes down, VM instances can be removed. The process can be manual, automated, or a combination of both.

Containers and Kubernetes

Container Instances and Azure Kubernetes Service are Azure compute resources that you can use to deploy and manage containers. Containers are lightweight, virtualized application environments. They're designed to be quickly created, scaled out, and stopped dynamically. You can run multiple instances of a containerized application on a single host machine.

App Service

With Azure App Service, you can quickly build, deploy, and scale enterprise-grade web, mobile, and API apps running on any platform. You can meet rigorous performance, scalability, security, and compliance requirements while using a fully managed platform to perform infrastructure maintenance. App Service is a platform as a service (PaaS) offering.

Functions

Functions are ideal when you're concerned only about the code running your service and not the underlying platform or infrastructure. They're commonly used when you need to perform work in response to an event (often via a REST request), timer, or message from another Azure service, and when that work can be completed quickly, within seconds or less.

Decide when to use Azure Virtual Machines

With Azure Virtual Machines, you can create and use VMs in the cloud. VMs provide infrastructure as a service (IaaS) in the form of a virtualized server and can be used in many ways. Just like a physical computer, you can customize all of the software running on the VM. VMs are an ideal choice when you need:

  • Total control over the operating system (OS).
  • The ability to run custom software.
  • To use custom hosting configurations.

An Azure VM gives you the flexibility of virtualization without having to buy and maintain the physical hardware that runs the VM. You still need to configure, update, and maintain the software that runs on the VM.

You can create and provision a VM in minutes when you select a preconfigured VM image. Selecting an image is one of the most important decisions you'll make when you create a VM. An image is a template used to create a VM. These templates already include an OS and often other software, like development tools or web hosting environments.

Examples of when to use VMs

  • During testing and development. VMs provide a quick and easy way to create different OS and application configurations. Test and development personnel can then easily delete the VMs when they no longer need them.

  • When running applications in the cloud. The ability to run certain applications in the public cloud as opposed to creating a traditional infrastructure to run them can provide substantial economic benefits. For example, an application might need to handle fluctuations in demand. Shutting down VMs when you don't need them or quickly starting them up to meet a sudden increase in demand means you pay only for the resources you use.

  • When extending your datacenter to the cloud. An organization can extend the capabilities of its own on-premises network by creating a virtual network in Azure and adding VMs to that virtual network. Applications like SharePoint can then run on an Azure VM instead of running locally. This arrangement makes it easier or less expensive to deploy than in an on-premises environment.

  • During disaster recovery. As with running certain types of applications in the cloud and extending an on-premises network to the cloud, you can get significant cost savings by using an IaaS-based approach to disaster recovery. If a primary datacenter fails, you can create VMs running on Azure to run your critical applications and then shut them down when the primary datacenter becomes operational again.

Move to the cloud with VMs

VMs are also an excellent choice when you move from a physical server to the cloud (also known as lift and shift). You can create an image of the physical server and host it within a VM with little or no changes. Just like a physical on-premises server, you must maintain the VM. You update the installed OS and the software it runs.

Scale VMs in Azure

You can run single VMs for testing, development, or minor tasks. Or you can group VMs together to provide high availability, scalability, and redundancy. No matter what your uptime requirements are, Azure has several features that can meet them. These features include:

  • Virtual machine scale sets
  • Azure Batch

What are virtual machine scale sets?

Virtual machine scale sets let you create and manage a group of identical, load-balanced VMs. Imagine you're running a website that enables scientists to upload astronomy images that need to be processed. If you duplicated the VM, you'd normally need to configure an additional service to route requests between multiple instances of the website. Virtual machine scale sets could do that work for you.

Scale sets allow you to centrally manage, configure, and update a large number of VMs in minutes to provide highly available applications. The number of VM instances can automatically increase or decrease in response to demand or a defined schedule. With virtual machine scale sets, you can build large-scale services for areas such as compute, big data, and container workloads.

What is Azure Batch?

Azure Batch enables large-scale parallel and high-performance computing (HPC) batch jobs with the ability to scale to tens, hundreds, or thousands of VMs.

When you're ready to run a job, Batch does the following:

  • Starts a pool of compute VMs for you.
  • Installs applications and staging data.
  • Runs jobs with as many tasks as you have.
  • Identifies failures.
  • Requeues work.
  • Scales down the pool as work completes.

There might be situations in which you need raw computing power or supercomputer-level compute power. Azure provides these capabilities.
Items.Author: yaswanthteja

Items.Title: 为微服务（NodeJS）打包可重用代码 | Packaging Reusable Code for Micro-services (NodeJS)
Items.Description:
封面照片由赵家伟在Unsplash上拍摄

假设您正在构建一个分布在多个微服务中的产品。 一般来说，微服务的概念是独立开发，但仍然可能有一些代码部分你会倾向于在不同的微服务中使用。

一种天真的方法是在特定的微服务中编写一段代码，然后将其复制到所有其他微服务中。 然而，这会导致不一致，这是由于在不同的微服务中使用不同版本的代码造成的。 换句话说，维护这样的代码库是很困难的。

使用 git 子模块，您可以在代码库中设置一个目录，其中包含指向另一个 git 存储库的链接。 您可以在独立的 git 存储库中编写代码的可重用部分，并将其用作所有 micr-services 中的子模块。

然而，许多开发人员只是不喜欢使用 git 子模块。 造成这种情况的原因有很多，从糟糕的开发经验到部署时遇到的困难。

您可能想在此处详细阅读它们

套餐

包是我们在项目中安装和导入的小型代码库，以使我们的生活更轻松！ 我们不会从头开始编写所有代码。 对于某些东西，我们更喜欢包提供的抽象。

公共包帮助我们开发我们的服务。 但是，我们也可以创建自己的包。 我们可以将我们的可重用代码打包为一个库，并轻松地在我们的任何微服务中使用它。

在这篇文章中，我将演示如何打包 ExpressJS 中间件并在微服务中使用它

让我们开始吧！

就像Linus Torvalds所说：

说话便宜。 给我看代码。

不喜欢跟进？ 直接前往 GitHub 存储库：

封面照片由赵家伟在Unsplash上拍摄

什么是可重用代码？

假设您正在构建一个分布在多个微服务中的产品。 一般来说，微服务的概念是独立开发，但仍然可能有一些代码部分你会倾向于在不同的微服务中使用。

一种天真的方法是在特定的微服务中编写一段代码，然后将其复制到所有其他微服务中。 然而，这会导致不一致，这是由于在不同的微服务中使用不同版本的代码造成的。 换句话说，维护这样的代码库是很困难的。

使用 git 子模块，您可以在代码库中设置一个目录，其中包含指向另一个 git 存储库的链接。 您可以在独立的 git 存储库中编写代码的可重用部分，并将其用作子模块……

中间件（可重用代码部分）

根据需要引导 NodeJS 项目并安装所需的依赖项。
这是一个基本的身份验证中间件，用于验证 JWT 令牌、对其进行解码并在请求中设置_id ：

 // authentication.js const jwt = require ( " jsonwebtoken " ); module . exports = ( req , res , next ) => { if ( req . headers . authorization && req . headers . authorization . startsWith ( " Bearer " ) ) { try { const token = req . headers . authorization . split ( " " )[ 1 ]; const decoded = jwt . verify ( token , " myjwtsecretthatnobodyknows " ); req . user_id = decoded . _id ; next (); } catch ( err ) { return res . status ( 401 ) . json ({ error : " Token expired or couldn't be verified! " }); } } else { return res . status ( 401 ). json ({ error : " Unauthorized: No token found " }); } };

如果我们计划扩展它们，还添加一个index.js文件，这将有助于管理多个中间件！

 // index.js const authentication = require ( " ./authentication " ); module . exports = { authentication , };

您会看到这个包没有正式的任何入口点或初始化的快速服务器。 我们甚至没有在这里安装或使用express 。 这是因为我们只是关注和开发中间件作为一个独立的包，而不是这里的整个服务器

最后通过 package.json 文件完成项目：

 // package.json { "name" : "@prc/middlewares" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "main" : "index.js" , "scripts" : { "pack" : "npm pack --pack-destination ../packages" }, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "dependencies" : { "jsonwebtoken" : "^8.5.1" }
}

这样做是为了确定一个包的范围。 通常，这设置为您组织的名称。 在此处阅读有关范围包的详细信息

  • 随意将pack-destination设置到本地系统中的任何位置，但是请注意它，因为我们稍后会需要它！

    • 为简单起见，我将使用此脚本生成一个 tarball 并从中安装。 在专业环境中，包通常发布到注册表（阅读下面有关发布的更多信息）！

    从项目目录运行以下命令以生成包的 tarball：

    您会看到 tarball 生成为： prc-middlewares-1.0.0.tgz 。

    引导一个 ExpressJS 项目。 安装express和其他必需的依赖项。

    在package.json文件中添加中间件包作为依赖项，如下所示：

     { "name" : "user" , "version" : "1.0.0" , "description" : "" , "main" : "server.js" , "scripts" : { "test" : "echo \" Error: no test specified \" && exit 1" }, "keywords" : [], "author" : "" , "license" : "ISC" , "dependencies" : { "@prc/middlewares" : "file:../packages/prc-middlewares-2.0.0.tgz" , "express" : "^4.18.1" , "jsonwebtoken" : "^8.5.1" }
}

    依赖@prc/middlewares设置为打包的 tarball 文件的文件路径（在上一步中已注明）。 这应该是一个相对路径并以file:

    注意：使用已发布的包时，您可能需要指定包版本、git 存储库或包注册表详细信息！

    确保在此之后运行安装：

    最后，服务器应该看起来像这样：

     // server.js const express = require ( " express " ); const { authentication } = require ( " @prc/middlewares " ); const jwt = require ( " jsonwebtoken " ); const app = express (); app . use ( express . json ()); app . post ( " /login " , ( req , res ) => { const { username , password } = req . body ; if ( username === " kinjal " && password === " 123456 " ) { const token = jwt . sign ({ _id : 4 }, " myjwtsecretthatnobodyknows " ); return res . json ({ token }); } else { return res . status ( 401 ). json ({ msg : " incorrect credentials! " }); } }); app . get ( " /user " , authentication , ( req , res ) => { return res . json ({ msg : " Hello there! " , user_id : req . user_id , }); }); const PORT = process . env . PORT || 8080 ; app . listen ( PORT , console . log ( `Server is running on port ${ PORT } 🚀` ));

    请注意我们如何像任何其他包一样require @prc/middlewares并在/user路由中使用authentication中间件！

    出版

    在与多个开发人员一起开发一堆微服务时，在本地打包库并不能解决任何问题。 相反，开发人员通常更喜欢版本控制和发布包。 包通常使用 npm、GitHub 或有时在组织的防火墙后面（如 AWS CodeArtifact）作为私有包发布到注册表

    这是一篇关于发布私有 NPM 包的精彩文章：
    创建和发布私有包 | 您可能还需要在单个项目中使用多个注册表。 要了解详细信息，请参阅：
    从多个注册表加载 npm 依赖项 | 埃克赫



    原文:

    Cover Photo by Jiawei Zhao on Unsplash

    Let's say you're building a product which is spread across multiple micro-services. Generally the notion of micro-services is to develop independently, still there might be some parts of code which you'll tend to use in different micro-services as it is.

    A naive approach would be writing a piece of code in a particular micro-service and then replicating it into all the others. However this would lead to an inconsistency which is caused be using different versions of the code across different micro-services. In other words, maintaining such a code base would be difficult.

    Git Submodules?

    Using git submodules, you can set up a directory within your codebase with links up to another git repository. You can write the reusable parts of the code in a standalone git repository and use it as a submodule in all your micr-services.

    However, many developer just do not prefer using git submodules. There are various reasons for it, starting from poor development experience to struggles while deploying.

    You might want to read them in details here

    Packages

    Packages are small libraries of code which we install and import in our project to make our lives easier! We do not code everything from scratch. For some stuffs, we prefer the abstraction that a package offers.

    A popular package manager and registry among Javascript developers is npm

    Public packages help us develop our services. However, we can also create our own packages. We can package our reusable code as a library and use it in any of our micro-services easily.

    In this post, I would be demonstrating how to package an ExpressJS middleware and use it in a micro-service

    Lets get started!

    Like Linus Torvalds said:

    Talk is cheap. Show me the code.

    Not a fan of following through? Head to the GitHub Repository straightway:

    Cover Photo by Jiawei Zhao on Unsplash

    What's reusable code?

    Let's say you're building a product which is spread across multiple micro-services. Generally the notion of micro-services is to develop independently, still there might be some parts of code which you'll tend to use in different micro-services as it is.

    A naive approach would be writing a piece of code in a particular micro-service and then replicating it into all the others. However this would lead to an inconsistency which is caused be using different versions of the code across different micro-services. In other words, maintaining such a code base would be difficult.

    Git Submodules?

    Using git submodules, you can set up a directory within your codebase with links up to another git repository. You can write the reusable parts of the code in a standalone git repository and use it as a submodule…

    Middlewares (Reusable code part)

    Bootstrap a NodeJS project as you want and install the dependencies required.
    Herein is a basic authentication middleware that verifies a JWT token, decodes it and set's the _id in the request:

    // authentication.js const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken"); module.exports = (req, res, next) => { if ( req.headers.authorization && req.headers.authorization.startsWith("Bearer") ) { try { const token = req.headers.authorization.split(" ")[1]; const decoded = jwt.verify(token, "myjwtsecretthatnobodyknows"); req.user_id = decoded._id; next(); } catch (err) { return res .status(401) .json({ error: "Token expired or couldn't be verified!" }); } } else { return res.status(401).json({ error: "Unauthorized: No token found" }); }
};

    Also adding an index.js file that would help managing multiple middlewares if we plan to extend them!

    // index.js const authentication = require("./authentication"); module.exports = { authentication,
};

    You'll see that this package doesn't officially have any entry point or an express server initialized. We didn't even install or use the express here. This is because we're just focusing and developing the middlewares as a standalone package and not the entire server here

    Finally the package.json file to complete the project:

    // package.json { "name": "@prc/middlewares", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "index.js", "scripts": { "pack": "npm pack --pack-destination ../packages" }, "keywords": [], "author": "", "license": "ISC", "dependencies": { "jsonwebtoken": "^8.5.1" }
}

    A few things to look out in the package.json file:

    • The name field: See how we prefixed the name with @prc. This is done to scope a package. Generally this is set to the name of your organization. Read in details about scoped packages here
    • The pack script: Used to package the pack the npm library into a tarball. Feel free to set the pack-destination to anywhere in your local system, however take note of it as we'll need that later!

    For sake of simplicity, I'll use this script to generate a tarball and install from it. In a professional setting, packages are generally published to a registry (Read more about publishing below)!

    Run the following command from the project directory to generate the tarball for the package:

    You'll see that the tarball is generated as: prc-middlewares-1.0.0.tgz.

    ExpressJS server (Micro-service)

    Bootstrap a ExpressJS project. Install express and other required dependencies.

    Add the middlewares package as dependencies in the package.json file as shown here:

    { "name": "user", "version": "1.0.0", "description": "", "main": "server.js", "scripts": { "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" }, "keywords": [], "author": "", "license": "ISC", "dependencies": { "@prc/middlewares": "file:../packages/prc-middlewares-2.0.0.tgz", "express": "^4.18.1", "jsonwebtoken": "^8.5.1" }
}

    The dependency @prc/middlewares is set as the file path of the packaged tarball file (which was noted in the previous step). This should be a relative path and prefixed with file:

    Note: When using a published package, you might need to specify the package version, git repository or package registry details!

    Make sure you run install after this:

    Finally the server should look something like this:

    // server.js const express = require("express");
const { authentication } = require("@prc/middlewares");
const jwt = require("jsonwebtoken"); const app = express();
app.use(express.json()); app.post("/login", (req, res) => { const { username, password } = req.body; if (username === "kinjal" && password === "123456") { const token = jwt.sign({ _id: 4 }, "myjwtsecretthatnobodyknows"); return res.json({ token }); } else { return res.status(401).json({ msg: "incorrect credentials!" }); }
}); app.get("/user", authentication, (req, res) => { return res.json({ msg: "Hello there!", user_id: req.user_id, });
}); const PORT = process.env.PORT || 8080;
app.listen(PORT, console.log(`Server is running on port ${PORT} 🚀`));

    Note how we require the @prc/middlewares like any other package and use the authentication middleware in the /user route!

    Publishing

    While developing a bunch of micro-services with multiple developers, packaging libraries locally doesn't solve anything. Instead, developers often prefer versioning and publishing the packages. Packages are generally published to registries as private packages using npm, GitHub or sometimes behind an organization's firewall (like AWS CodeArtifact)

    Here's an amazing article to get started with publishing private NPM packages:
    Creating and publishing private packages | npm Docs

    You might also need to work with multiple registries in a single project. Scoped packages come in handy in such situations. To get into details, refer:
    Loading npm dependencies from multiple registries | Eckher
Items.Author: kinjalrk2k

Items.Title: 如何进入大科技 | How to get into big tech
Items.Description:
现在我是 Uber 的 SWE 2，之前我是 Flipkart 的 UI 2，所以这是我对收到的几个问题的看法。

如果您不是这种情况，那么让您涉足一个体面的品牌也有帮助。

从我个人的经验来看，一旦我进入 Flipkart，我就接到了来自知名公司的面试电话，这是我之前没有接到的。

现在闯入第一家知名公司是许多人的诀窍。 为此，我建议从所有渠道申请

  1. 申请公司页面上的职位发布
  2. 申请瑙克里
  3. 申请其他工作门户
  4. 直接ping HR
  5. 直接 ping 工程经理，他们中的许多人说他们正在为他们的团队招聘。
  6. 在您的目标公司工作的 Ping 工程师。

第 4、5、6 点可能会为您带来推荐，这将有助于您获得面试。

总结一下，我觉得拿到面试电话也需要一点运气，所以继续努力。

特别是DS问题？

例如对于 FE（前端工程），它可能是机器编码轮（他们可能会要求您构建一些项目）、 javascript 轮（他们将评估您的 FE 技能）、设计和架构轮（他们将测试您的系统设计技能） ），编码轮（他们可能会问你基于 DS 算法的问题），最后是经理轮。

对于基于 DS 算法的问题，我只是做了尽可能多的问题（在 2-3 个月的时间内大约 80 个）leetcode 媒体。
只要确保您触及热门话题并涵盖一些广度即可。 这在大多数情况下应该足够了。



原文:

Right now I am SWE 2 at Uber and previously I was a UI 2 at Flipkart, so here is my opinion on a few questions I receive.

Q) How do you get interview calls?

Ans)From my personal experience, having a good college background helps in the initial years. If that is not the case for you, then getting you foot into one of the decent size brands also helps.

From my personal experience, once I got into Flipkart, I was getting interview calls from renowned companies that I was not getting earlier.

Now breaking into the first renowned company is the trick for many out there. For that I suggest to apply from all channels aka

  1. Apply on job postings on the company page
  2. Apply on Naukri
  3. Apply on other job portals
  4. Directly ping HRs
  5. Directly ping Engineering managers, many of them say they are hiring for their team.
  6. Ping engineers working on the company you are targeting.

Points 4,5,6 may get you referrals and that would help you get interviews.

Closing thought, I feel it requires a bit of luck as well to get the interview call, so keep trying.

Q) What kind of questions are asked during the interview? Specially DS questions?

Ans) Every specialization would have it's own set of rounds. For example for FE (frontend engineering) it maybe machine coding round (where they may ask you to build some project), javascript round (where they will assess your FE skills), design and architecture round (where they will test your system design skills), coding round(where they may ask you DS algo based questions) and finally a manager round.

For DS algo based questions, I just did as many questions I could do (around 80 within a time period of 2-3 months) of leetcode mediums.
Just make sure you touch the popular topics and cover some breadth. That should be enough for the most part.
Items.Author: bisvarup

Items.Title: 1 小时内从 Heroku 迁移到 AWS 的终极指南 | The ultimate guide to migrate from Heroku to AWS in 1 hour
Items.Description:
本指南介绍如何使用Qovery将在 Heroku 上运行的应用程序迁移到 AWS。 它涵盖了在 AWS 上部署应用程序并将数据从 Heroku Postgres 传输到 AWS 管理的数据库所需采取的所有步骤。

这是基于 AWS 的“类似 Heroku”的体验。

这是最终结果😎

假设👨‍🏫

费用💵

  • 迁移步骤🪜
    1. 使用 Buildpacks 或创建 Dockerfile
    2. 在 Qovery 上创建资源
    3. 配置环境变量和机密
    4. 将数据从 Heroku 数据库复制到 AWS 数据库
    5. 部署您的应用程序
    6. 这两个选项都构建了一个可由容器引擎（例如 Docker）运行的容器映像。 我将解释如何使用 Buildpacks。 请参阅原始指南以获取使用 Dockerfile 的说明。

      这是 支持的语言和框架列表。

      支持的语言

      语言版本
      节点.JS 任何
      任何
      任何
      爪哇任何
      摇篮任何
      虚拟机任何
      圣杯任何
      斯卡拉任何
      玩任何
      任何
      去任何

      您可以尝试使用 Qovery 和 Buildpacks 在 AWS 上部署您的应用程序，如果您没有成功，我们建议您切换到 Docker。

      限制👀

      由于我们的 Buildpacks 实现，以下是一些限制：

      • 这些限制将在未来几个月内得到解决。

        应用



        脚步：

        1. 连接到 [Qovery 控制台][urls.start_qovery]。
        2. 创建您的 [Organization][docs.using-qovery.configuration.organization] 和您的 [Project][docs.using-qovery.configuration.project]。
        3. 创建一个名为production的环境（之后可以更改）。
        4. 创建一个应用程序并给它一个名字（如果你不知道，你可以给你的 repo 的名字）
        5. 从您的 GitHub、GitLab 或 Bitbucket 中选择您的应用程序存储库。
        6. 选择要部署的分支。
        7. 根据需要选择Buildpacks或Dockerfile的构建模式。
        8. 指定应用程序的本地侦听端口。
        9. 点击“创建”

        恭喜！ 您的应用程序已创建🎉

        您可以在部署之前配置 vCPU、内存、环境变量...。 如果您想在完成配置之前部署它，您可以单击“操作”>“部署”。

        如果您从单一存储库部署应用程序，我们在此处为您提供必读指南。

        数据库⚡️

        以下是部署数据库的步骤：



        脚步：

        1. 转到您的production环境。
        2. 通过单击“添加”>“数据库”添加您的数据库。
        3. 选择数据库（PostgreSQL、MySQL、MongoDB、Redis..）和您要部署的版本。
        4. 为您的数据库选择托管或容器模式。
        5. 选择Public可访问性（如果您不想从现有 Heroku 数据库恢复数据，请设置为Private ）。

        恭喜！ 您的数据库也已创建🎉

        如果您使用 MongoDB Atlas 或 AWS 上想要连接到 Qovery 部署的应用程序的现有数据库。 查看我们关于 VPC 对等互连以及如何安全连接到现有数据库的教程。

        基本上，Secret 类似于环境变量，但值是加密的，不能被泄露。 两者都在构建和运行应用程序期间作为环境变量注入。

        要从 Heroku 中提取环境变量，我们建议使用Heroku CLI并将所有环境变量和机密导出到 .env (dot env) 文件中。 否则，Qovery 将无法部署您的应用程序！

        通过 Heroku CLI 使用以下命令导出环境变量：

         # To install Heroku CLI: https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/heroku-cli heroku config GREETINGS: hello world STRIPE_API_KEY: xxx-yyy-zzz IS_PRODUCTION: true

        然后您可以通过 Web 界面创建环境变量（观看下面的视频）



        或通过Qovery CLI ：

         # auth yourself qovery auth # selection the app where you want to import your environment variables qovery context set # import your Heroku environment variables heroku config --app <your_heroku_app_name> --json | qovery env import --heroku-json Qovery: Heroku environment variables import from JSON ? Do you want to import Environment Variables or Secrets? Environment Variables ? What environment variables do you want to import? [ Use arrows to move, space to select , <right> to all, <left> to none, type to filter] [ x] GREETINGS = hello world [ ] STRIPE_API_KEY = xxx-yyy-zzz > [ x] IS_PRODUCTION = true Qovery: ✅ Environment Variables successfully imported!

        将您的前端应用程序连接到您的后端应用程序

        要将您的前端应用程序连接到您的后端应用程序，我们将创建一个 [环境变量别名][docs.using-qovery.configuration.environment-variable#alias-environment-variable]。

        以下是创建前端应用程序的方法：



        现在如何将您的前端应用程序与您的后端应用程序连接起来：



        您也可以查看此论坛回复以了解如何操作。

        将您的后端应用程序连接到您的数据库

        与将前端应用程序连接到后端应用程序一样，您可以使用_DATABASE_URL_INTERNAL创建一个环境变量别名DATABASE_URL用于内置秘密整理。

        我们终于准备好在 AWS 上部署我的应用程序了！



        看最终结果😎

        如何创建自定义域？

        查看有关 [如何配置您的自定义域][guides.getting-started.setting-custom-domain] 的文档。

        如何监控我的应用程序？

        我们建议使用Datadog或任何其他监控产品来监控 Qovery 部署的应用程序。 查看我们关于如何安装 Datadog 的教程

        你有 Heroku “Review App” 等价物吗？

        是的，这就是我们所说的 预览环境

        如何回滚？

        查看 应用回滚文档

        自动缩放如何工作？

        查看应用程序自动缩放文档

        如何管理数据库迁移？

        查看我们的论坛回复

        是否可以获得外壳/连接到我的应用程序？

        是的，使用 Qovery CLI 和命令qovery shell 。 查看文档。

        我可以使用 Terraform 和基础设施即代码吗？

        当然，我们有一个可用的Qovery Terraform 提供程序。

        如何将我的应用程序连接到 MongoDB Atlas？

        如果您使用 MongoDB Atlas，请查看我们关于 VPC 对等以及如何安全连接到现有 MongoDB Atlas 数据库的教程。

        如何将我的应用程序连接到不受 Qovery 管理的 AWS 服务？

        如果您想将您的应用程序连接到不受 Qovery 管理的 AWS 服务，请查看我们关于 VPC 对等互连以及如何安全地连接到此 AWS 服务的教程。

        如果您有关于 Qovery 的常见问题，我们提供了更一般的常见问题解答部分。

        结束👋🏼

        恭喜！ 您已从 Heroku 迁移到 AWS。 如果您有任何问题，请随时查看我们的论坛并打开一个线程。



原文:

This guide describes how to migrate your application running on Heroku to AWS with Qovery. It covers all required steps you need to take to deploy your application on AWS and transfer your data from Heroku Postgres to the database managed by AWS.

Qovery is a platform used by more than 22 000 developers to deploy their apps on their AWS account. It's the "Heroku-like" experience on top of AWS.

This is the final result 😎



Assumptions 👨‍🏫

Costs 💵

  • Qovery installs an EC2 instance (perfect for hobby project) or an EKS cluster (perfect for production) on your AWS account.
  • Qovery is free up to 100 deployments/month (see pricing)

Migration Steps 🪜

  1. Use Buildpacks or Create your Dockerfile
  2. Create resources on Qovery
  3. Configure Environment Variables and Secrets
  4. Copy data from your Heroku databases to your AWS databases
  5. Deploy your apps
  6. FAQ by Heroku users

1. Create your Dockerfile or Use Buildpacks 🍃

Qovery supports two ways to build and run your application coming from Heroku:

Both options build a container image that is runnable by a container engine (E.g. Docker). Qovery runs containers on Kubernetes.

I will explain how to use Buildpacks. Refer to the original guide to get instructions to use a Dockerfile.

Buildpacks automatically detects the language and the framework your application is using. Buildpacks builds and runs your app. Here is the list of supported languages and frameworks.

Supported languages

language version
Node.JS any
Clojure any
Python any
Java any
Gradle any
JVM any
Grails any
Scala any
Play any
PHP any
Go any

⚠️ We do recommend using Docker to keep the full control of what's going on behind the scene. Buildpacks is a great technology but difficult to debug when something goes wrong. You can try deploying your apps on AWS with Qovery with Buildpacks, if you do not succeed, we do recommend switching for Docker.

Limitations 👀

Here are some limitations due to our Buildpacks implementation:

  • Qovery Buildpacks does not support Procfile with multiple commands at the moment.
  • Qovery does not support custom Buildpacks.

Those limitations will be solved in the coming months.

2. Create resources on Qovery 👩‍💻

Application



Steps:

  1. Connect to the [Qovery console][urls.start_qovery].
  2. Create your [Organization][docs.using-qovery.configuration.organization] and your [Project][docs.using-qovery.configuration.project].
  3. Create an environment with the name production (it can be changed after).
  4. Create an application and give it a name (you can give the name of your repo if you have no idea)
  5. Select your app repository from your GitHub, GitLab or Bitbucket.
  6. Select the branch you want to deploy.
  7. Select the Build mode for Buildpacks or Dockerfile according to what you want.
  8. Specify the local listening port of your application.
  9. Click on "create"

Congrats! Your application is created 🎉

⚠️ Your application is created but not deployed yet! You can configure the vCPU, Memory, Environment Variables... before deploying it. If you want to deploy it before finishing the configuration you can click on "Actions" > "Deploy".

If you deploy an app from a mono-repository, we have a must-read guide for you here.

Database ⚡️

Here are the steps to deploy your database:



Steps:

  1. Go to your production environment.
  2. Add your database by clicking on "Add" > "Database".
  3. Select the database (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis..) and the version you want to deploy.
  4. Select Managed or Container mode for your database.
  5. Select Public accessibility (set Private if you don't want to restore your data from an existing Heroku database).

Congrats! Your database is created as well 🎉

If you use MongoDB Atlas, or an existing database on AWS that you want to connect to your application deployed by Qovery. Check out our tutorial about VPC peering and how to securely connect to your existing database.

3. Configure your Environment Variables and Secrets 🛡

Qovery makes the difference between an Environment Variable and a Secret. Basically, a Secret is similar to an Environment Variable but the value is encrypted and can't be revealed. Both are injected as environment variables during the build and the run of your applications.

To extract your environment variables from Heroku, we recommend using the Heroku CLI and exporting all the environment variables and secrets in an .env (dot env) file. Qovery supports the import of a dot env file via the Qovery web interface and the Qovery CLI.

⚠️ If you use Buildpacks for one of your app AND you have indicated a local listening port of your application, you will need to add an environment variable PORT with the value of your port to make your application starting properly. Otherwise, Qovery will fail to deploy your app!

Export your environment variable via the Heroku CLI with the command:

# To install Heroku CLI: https://devcenter.heroku.com/articles/heroku-cli
heroku config GREETINGS: hello world
STRIPE_API_KEY: xxx-yyy-zzz
IS_PRODUCTION: true

Then you can create your environment variables via the web interface (watch the video below)



Or via the Qovery CLI:

# auth yourself
qovery auth # selection the app where you want to import your environment variables
qovery context set # import your Heroku environment variables
heroku config --app <your_heroku_app_name> --json | qovery env import --heroku-json Qovery: Heroku environment variables import from JSON
? Do you want to import Environment Variables or Secrets? Environment Variables
? What environment variables do you want to import? [Use arrows to move, space to select, <right> to all, <left> to none, type to filter] [x] GREETINGS=hello world [ ] STRIPE_API_KEY=xxx-yyy-zzz
> [x] IS_PRODUCTION=true Qovery: ✅ Environment Variables successfully imported!

⚠️ Import sensitive data (E.g. API keys, credentials...) as Secret and not Environment Variable.

Connect your frontend app to your backend app

To connect your frontend app your backend app we will create an [environment variable alias][docs.using-qovery.configuration.environment-variable#alias-environment-variable].

Here is how to create a frontend app:



And now how to connect your frontend app with your backend app:



You can also take a look at this forum reply to learn how to do it.

Connect your backend app to your database

Same as connecting your frontend app to your backend app, you can create an environment variable alias DATABASE_URL for the built-in secret finishing with _DATABASE_URL_INTERNAL.

⚠️ Create an alias on _DATABASE_URL_INTERNAL and not _DATABASE_URL



4. Copy data from your Heroku databases to your AWS databases

Coming soon with Replibyte

5. Deploy your apps!

We are finally ready to deploy my applications on AWS!



Watch the final result 😎

https://www.loom.com/share/da31c21f9c104eae9270e4c4db59055e

FAQ by Heroku users

How to create a custom domain?

Check out the documentation on [how to configure your custom domain][guides.getting-started.setting-custom-domain].

How to monitor my apps?

We do recommend using Datadog or any other monitoring products for monitoring your apps deployed by Qovery. Check out our tutorial on how to install Datadog

Do you have Heroku "Review App" equivalent?

Yes, it's what we call Preview Environment

How to rollback?

Check out the app rollback documentation

How auto-scaling works?

Check out the app auto-scaling documentation

How to manage database migration?

Check out our forum reply

Is it possible to get a shell / connect to my app?

Yes, with the Qovery CLI and the command qovery shell. Check out the documentation.

Can I use Terraform and Infrastructure as Code?

Absolutely, we have a Qovery Terraform provider available.

How can I connect my app to MongoDB Atlas?

If you use MongoDB Atlas check out our tutorial about VPC peering and how to securely connect to your existing MongoDB Atlas database.

How can I connect my app to an AWS service not managed by Qovery?

If you want to connect your app to an AWS service not managed by Qovery, check out our tutorial about VPC peering and how to securely connect to this AWS service.

If you have a common question about Qovery, we have a more general FAQ section available.

Wrapping up 👋🏼

Congrats! You have migrated from Heroku to AWS. Feel free to check out our forum and open a thread if you have any question.
