Items.Title: 了解如何使用荷兰式拍卖模型以最优惠的价格出售您的 NFT | Learn how to sell your NFTs at the best price using the Dutch Auction Model
Items.Description:
用于UVLabs的Pari Tomar

发表于拍卖是一种流行的方法，可以以最优惠的价格出售 NFT 收藏品。

为了出售您的 NFT 收藏品，有几种拍卖技术，但荷兰式拍卖与其他技术不同，使用频率更高。

与常规拍卖不同，在荷兰式拍卖中，价格开始时非常高，然后以预定的时间间隔缓慢下降。

简而言之，在荷兰式拍卖中，NFT 的价格从初始价格（上限）开始，并定期下降固定数量（例如，每 10 分钟下降 0.1 ETH），直到达到它可以达到的最低价格（剩余的价格）

为什么荷兰式拍卖如此受欢迎？

原因之一可能与区块链上的天然气战争有关。

荷兰式拍卖风格用于将多余的铸币费从矿工转移到艺术家身上，或用于资助特定的慈善机构或社区 DAO。

所以没有时间了，让我们编写荷兰拍卖智能合约来出售我们的 NFT！

先决条件：

为了铸造我们的 NFT 并使用荷兰式拍卖出售它，我们将使用我们之前的一篇文章创建一个 NFT 合约，并从那里复制该合约。

参考本文或github repo获取NFT智能合约。

在您的Remix IDE中创建一个名为 NFT.sol 的新文件，然后粘贴我们在上面使用的合约。

现在我们可以走了！

让我们做一些荷兰式拍卖😎

在您的 Remix IDE 中创建一个新的 Solidity 文件并将其命名为 DutchAuction.sol

创建一个 IERC-721 接口，然后初始化您的荷兰式拍卖合同。

使用以下代码作为参考：

 // SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT pragma solidity ^0.8.10; interface IERC721 { function transferFrom( address _from, address _to, uint _nftId ) external; } contract DutchAuction { }

初始化合约后，我们将定义一些必须在合约中使用的变量。

  • 首先，我们将定义拍卖的持续时间。 这意味着 NFT 的拍卖将在该期限后到期。 我们将定义 2 天的持续时间。

  • 接下来，我们需要存储 NFT 的地址及其 ID。 因此，我们将使用 IERC721 接口来存储 NFT。

我们使这个变量不可变，这样一旦部署合约，它的值就不会改变。

  • 然后，我们将存储卖家的地址。 因此，我们将定义将存储卖方地址的变量卖方。

  • 荷兰式拍卖需要起拍价。 所以我们要定义一个变量startingPrice

  • 我们还需要拍卖开始和拍卖结束的时间戳。 因此，我们将定义两个变量 startAt 和 expiresAt 来存储各自的值。

  • 最后，我们将定义 discountRate。 解决公共不可变卖方； 接下来，我们将在构造函数中初始化这些状态变量。

    在其中，我们将传入_startingPrice、_discountRate、_nft和_nftId。

     constructor( uint _startingPrice, uint _discountRate, address _nft, uint _nftId ) { }

    • 在构造函数内部，我们将卖方设置为该合约的部署者。 并且一旦 NFT 被出售，我们需要将 ETH 转移给卖家，因此我们将使其支付。

      卖家=应付（msg.sender）；

    • 我们将获取用户的输入以获取startingPrice、discountRate、startAt 和expireAt 。 因此，我们将其写为：

      起始价格 = _起始价格； 折扣率 = _discountRate; 我们还将检查 NFT 的价格是否始终大于零。

      最后，我们使用输入设置 NFT。 所以我们可以说：

      现在，因为我们的构造函数已经完成。

      接下来，我们将定义两个我们将进一步使用的函数： currentPrice()和buyNow()

      • 使用currentPrice() ，我们将知道 NFT 在特定时间实例的价格。

      为此，我们将编写：

       function currentPrice() public view returns (uint) { uint timeElapsed = block.timestamp - startAt; uint discount = discountRate * timeElapsed; return startingPrice - discount; }

      • 接下来，使用buyNow()函数，用户将能够购买 NFT。

        在这个函数中，首先，我们将检查拍卖是否过期。

        如果拍卖没有过期，那么我们将获取当前价格并使用 currentPrice 函数将其存储在价格变量中。

        接下来，我们将检查我们发送的金额是否大于或等于价格。

        一旦我们知道 msg.sender 已经发送了足够的 ETH 来购买 NFT，我们将使用 3 个参数转移 NFT 的所有权： seller、msg.sender、nftId。

        最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们使用了selfdestruct()函数来结束拍卖。 因此，我们将在函数内部传递卖家地址。

        你猜怎么了？

        我们已经完成了我们的荷兰式拍卖智能合约！！ 你的合同应该是这样的👇

        接下来，我们将通过出售我们的 NFT 来测试我们的荷兰式拍卖智能合约。 还记得我们在本文开头创建了一个文件 NFT.sol 吗？

        现在我们将部署该 NFT，然后使用其 tokenURI 调用 mint 函数。

        一旦我们铸造了我们的演示 NFT。 接下来我们将部署我们的荷兰式拍卖合约。

        编译荷兰式拍卖合约，然后部署它。

        为了部署我们的合约，我们必须传递一些参数。

        我们将得到startingPrice为1000000， discountRate为1*，*我们刚刚部署的NFT的地址，最后，我们必须提供等于1的***tokenId *。

        添加参数后，点击 Deploy 按钮来部署我们的合约。

        一旦我们的荷兰式拍卖合同被部署。 接下来我们要做的是批准荷兰式拍卖来使用我们的 NFT。

        为此，我们将在 Deployed Contracts 下打开我们的 NFT 并执行批准功能。

        我们需要传递荷兰拍卖的部署地址和 NFT 的 tokenId 来运行批准功能。

        所以现在我们的荷兰拍卖已经开始了！！

        要检查荷兰式拍卖是否已经开始，请打开部署的荷兰式拍卖合约并多次运行 currentPrice 函数。 您会注意到每次运行该函数时，您都会得到一个递减的价格值。

        现在是时候出售我们的 NFT 了。

        首先，运行currentPrice函数并复制价格值。

        然后切换到您想从中购买 NFT 的其他帐户，并将价格值粘贴到value选项下。

        然后运行​​buyNow函数。

        你猜怎么了！？

        您刚刚使用荷兰式拍卖出售了您的 NFT！ 如果你回到你的 NFT 合约并运行ownerOf函数，你可以看到合约的所有者被更新了。

        这就是我们如何使用荷兰式拍卖出售我们的 NFT ！！！

        如果您喜欢这篇文章，请给我们鼓掌。 不用担心，如果您收到任何错误消息，可以通过在 Twitter 上标记@uv_labs来询问我们。

        作者（欢迎反馈）：👇

        克里斯蒂安·弗朗西斯科·梅奥诺·卡内尔 - 马切伊·斯莫林斯基 - May 20 5月20日



原文:

Pari Tomar for UVLabs

Posted on May 21

Auctions are a popular method to sell out the NFT collection drops at the best price possible.

There are a couple of auction techniques present in order to sell your NFT collection, but Dutch Auction is different from other techniques and is used more often.

Unlike a regular auction, in Dutch Auction, prices start very high and then slowly drop at predetermined time intervals.

In simpler words, in a Dutch Auction, the price of an NFT starts at an initial price (ceiling) and drops by a fixed amount periodically (eg. 0.1 ETH every 10 min) until it hits the lowest price it can go (the resting price)

Why Dutch Auction is so popular?

One of the reasons can be linked to the gas wars on the blockchain.

Gas wars are the spike in the blockchain gas fees that occurs when a high volume of transactions is initiated around the same time on blockchains like Ethereum, which has a low transaction throughput.

The Dutch auction style is used to redirect the excess minting fee from the miners towards the artists or channeled to fund specific charities or a community DAO.

So without anymore time, let’s write the Dutch Auction smart contract to sell our NFT!

Prerequisites:

In order to mint our NFT and sell it using Dutch Auction, we are going to create an NFT contract using one of our previous articles, and copy the contract from there.

Refer to this article or github repo to get the NFT smart contract.

Create a new file in your Remix IDE named as NFT.sol and paste the contract we used above.

Now we are good to go!

Let’s do some Dutch Auction 😎

1. Write our Smart Contract

Create a new solidity file in your Remix IDE and name it as DutchAuction.sol

Create an IERC-721 interface and then initialize your Dutch Auction contract.

Use the following code for reference:

// SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
pragma solidity ^0.8.10;

interface IERC721 {
    function transferFrom(
        address _from,
        address _to,
        uint _nftId
    ) external;
}
contract DutchAuction { }

Once you’ve initialized your contract, we are going to define some variables that we have to use in our contract.

  • First, we are going to define the duration of the auction. That means the auction for the NFT will be expired after that duration. And we will define the duration for 2 days.

  • Next, we need to store the address of the NFT and its ID. Therefore, we will use IERC721 interface to store the NFT.

We made this variable immutable so that its value does not change once the contract is deployed.

  • Then, we going to store the address of the seller. Thus we will define the variable seller which will be storing the seller’s address.

  • The Dutch Auction needs a starting price. So we are going to define a variable startingPrice

  • We also need the timestamp of when the auction starts and when the auction ends. Therefore, we will define 2 variables, startAt and expiresAt which will store the respective values.

  • Lastly, we will define the discountRate. DiscountRate will determine the rate at which the price decrements, starting from the startingPrice.

    uint private constant DURATION = 2 days; IERC721 public immutable nft; uint public immutable nftId; address public immutable seller; uint public immutable startingPrice; uint public immutable startAt; uint public immutable expiresAt;

    uint public immutable discountRate;

Next, we will initialize these state variables in a constructor.

Inside it, we are going to pass in the _startingPrice, _discountRate, _nft, and _nftId.

constructor(
        uint _startingPrice,
        uint _discountRate,
        address _nft,
        uint _nftId
    ) { }

  • Inside the constructor, we will set the seller to the deployer of this contract. And once the NFT will be sold, we need to transfer the ETH to the seller, therefore we will make it payable.

    seller = payable(msg.sender);

  • We will take the input from the user to get the startingPrice, discountRate, startAt and expireAt. So, we will write it as:

    startingPrice = _startingPrice; discountRate = _discountRate; startAt = block.timestamp;

    expiresAt = block.timestamp + DURATION;

  • We will also check whether the price of the NFT is always greater than zero or not.

    _startingPrice >= _discountRate * DURATION, "Starting Price is less than the discount offered"

_discountRate * DURATION is the maximum amount of price deduction that is applied to the startingPrice

  • Lastly, we set the NFT using the input. So we can say:

    nft = IERC721(_nft);
    nftId = _nftId;

Now since our constructor is complete.

Next, we’re going to define 2 functions that we will be using further: currentPrice() and buyNow()

  • Using currentPrice(), we will know the price of the NFT at that particular instance of time.

For that, we will write:

function currentPrice() public view returns (uint) {
        uint timeElapsed = block.timestamp - startAt;
        uint discount = discountRate * timeElapsed;
        return startingPrice - discount;
}

  • Next, using the buyNow() function, the user will be able to buy the NFT.

    function buy() external payable { require(block.timestamp < expiresAt, "Auction expired! :("); uint price = currentPrice(); require(msg.value >= price, "ETH is less than the price"); nft.transferFrom(seller, msg.sender, nftId); selfdestruct(seller);

    }

In this function, first, we’ll check whether the auction is expired or not.

If the auction is not expired, then we will get the current price and store it inside the price variable using the currentPrice function.

Next, we will check that the amount we have sent is greater than or equal to the price.

Once we know that the msg.sender has sent enough ETH to buy the NFT, we will transfer the ownership of the NFT using the 3 parameters: seller, msg.sender, nftId.

Last but not the least, we have used the selfdestruct() function to close the auction. Therefore, we will pass the seller address inside the function.

Guess what?

We have completed our Dutch Auction Smart Contract!! 🎊

Your contract should look like this 👇

Next, we’re going to test our Dutch Auction smart contract by selling our NFT. 😮

2. Mint our NFT using its tokenURI

Remember we created a file NFT.sol at the first place of this article?

Now we will deploy that NFT and then call the mint function using its tokenURI.

Once we have minted our demo NFT. Next we are going to deploy our Dutch Auction contract.

3. Deploy the Dutch Auction contract

Compile the Dutch Auction contract and then deploy it.

There are some parameters we have to pass in order to deploy our contract.

We will get the startingPrice to 1000000, discountRate at 1*,* the address of the NFT which we deployed just a while before and lastly, we have to provide the ***tokenId* which is equal to 1.

Once you’ve added the parameters, hit the Deploy button to deploy our contract.

Once our Dutch Auction contract is deployed. Next thing we’re going to do is approve the Dutch Auction to spend our NFT.

For that, we will open our NFT under Deployed Contracts and execute the approve function.

We need to pass the Dutch Auction’s deployed address and the tokenId of the NFT to run the approve function.

So now our DUTCH AUCTION HAS STARTED!!

To check whether the Dutch Auction has started or not, open the deployed Dutch Auction contract and run the currentPrice function several times. You’ll notice everytime you run the function, you’re getting a decremented price value.

Now its time to sell our NFT.

4. Sell our NFT using Dutch Auction contract

First, run the currentPrice function and copy the price value.

And then switch to some other account, from which you want to buy the NFT and paste the price value under the value option.

And then run the buyNow function.

GUESS WHAT!?

You just sold your NFT using Dutch Auction!! 😍

If you go back to your NFT contract and run the ownerOf function, you can see the owner of the contract is updated.

And this is how we can sell our NFT using the Dutch Auction!!!

If you liked the article, do give us a clap. 👏

And don’t worry, if you get any error message, you can ask us by tagging @uv_labs on Twitter.

Authors (open to feedback): 👇

Amateur-Dev and Pari Tomar

Cristian Francisco Meoño Canel - May 20

Maciej Smoliński - May 20
Items.Author: paritomarrr

Items.Title: 使用新 Kotlin 运行时的云函数 | Cloud Functions Using the New Kotlin Runtime
Items.Description:
在本文中，我们将了解如何使用 Kotlin 运行时编写 Cloud Functions。 如果您是 Android 开发人员，您基本上可以构建您的应用程序并编写任何必要的云功能，而无需学习任何新语言！ 如果缺少任何内容，您可以使用您喜欢的后端语言扩展 Appwrite。

在我们开始之前，有几个先决条件。 如果您已经设置了先决条件，则可以跳到以下部分。

为了跟进，您需要事先做一些事情。

如果您还没有设置 Appwrite 实例，您可以按照入门指南快速启动和运行。 您可以在 DigitalOcean 上的一键安装或使用 Docker 的手动安装之间进行选择。

服务器启动并运行后，转到服务器公共 IP 地址上的 Appwrite 仪表板（或本地安装，如果您在本地安装）并创建一个新用户帐户。

我们将在本练习中使用 Appwrite CLI，因为它使过程超级简单。 如果你安装了 Node.js，安装命令很简单

npm install -g appwrite-cli

如果你不喜欢npm ，我们有许多安装选项，你可以在CLI 的入门指南中找到。

一切准备就绪，我们现在可以开始了！ 使用以下命令登录到 Appwrite CLI，并使用我们在设置 Appwrite 服务器时使用的凭据进行登录。

 appwrite login ? Enter your email test @test.com ? Enter your password ******** ✓ Success

接下来，我们需要创建一个新的 Appwrite 项目来使用。 我们可以使用以下命令进行设置

appwrite init project ? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project ? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project ✓ Success

您可以为您的项目指定您选择的任何名称。 在这种情况下，我将坚持使用默认值。 您会注意到当前目录中有一个新的appwrite.json文件，其中存储了有关您的项目的所有信息。

是时候开始编写我们的函数了！ 但是等等，我们不需要从头开始！ 为您的函数命名并选择Kotlin 1.6运行时。 这将在您的项目中创建一个新的 Appwrite 函数并设置所有必要的样板代码。 随意检查在functions/kotlin-example目录中创建的文件。 您会发现 CLI 创建了一个简单的 Kotlin 函数，该函数返回一条非常重要的 JSON 消息

{ "areDevelopersAwesome" : true
}

在我们继续修改函数之前，让我们部署它以了解从初始化函数到部署它的端到端工作流程。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

在此期间，将安装所有函数的依赖项，并为您的函数生成一个.jar文件。

您可以前往 Appwrite Dashboard 跟踪部署进度。

部署完成后，您可以通过单击立即执行按钮来执行您的功能。

第一次执行导致我们称之为冷启动。 本质上这是第一次创建运行时，因此需要更长的时间。 如您所见，第一次执行大约需要 450毫秒，后续执行大约需要4 毫秒。 现在我们已经了解了完整的工作流程，我们可以开始修改代码并编写一些很酷的函数。

正如标题所示，我们将编写一个云函数来回答数学中最基本的问题之一。

给定的数字是奇数还是偶数？ 我知道你在想什么……太简单了！ 为什么我需要一个云功能？ 我可以写

fun main () { val x = 12 println ( x % 2 == 0 ) // true println ( ( x / 2 ) * 2 == x ) // true println ( x and 1 == 0 ) // true println ( ( x shr 1 ) shl 1 == x ) // true }

可是等等！ 我们正在将此提升到一个新的水平。 我们将使用目前最复杂且编写良好的 API 之一来决定我们的数字是偶数还是奇数！ 撇开玩笑不谈，这个示例旨在说明如何从 Cloud Function 进行 API 调用，这将使您能够构建任何您想要的东西。

首先，在您喜欢的 IDE 中打开kotlin-example文件夹。 让我们从一个空白画布开始，清理我们的src/Index.kt文件以

@Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { return res . json ( mapOf ( "message" to "Hello, World!" )) }

为了更轻松地处理 JSON 对象，我们将使用流行的[gson](https://github.com/google/gson)库。 在deps.gradle文件中包含以下依赖项。

 dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0' }

接下来，让我们编写一些代码来读取和解析作为参数发送给该函数的数字。

 import com.google.gson.Gson private val gson = Gson () @Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( req . payload . ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap :: class . java ) val number = payload [ "number" ] ?: 2 return res . json ( mapOf ( "message" to "Hello, World!" )) }

让我们回顾一下我们刚刚编写的代码。 从函数文档中，我们看到payload可通过request对象获得，并且是一个 JSON 字符串，需要将其转换为 JSON 对象才能进一步解析。 如果payload为空，我们将其替换为空的 JSON 对象。

然后我们检索传递给函数的number ，如果它为空，则默认为数字2 。 我们还导入gson库并创建它的一个实例。

接下来，让我们使用本机 HTTP 客户端调用 API。

 import com.google.gson.Gson import java.io.BufferedReader import java.io.InputStreamReader import java.net.HttpURLConnection import java.net.URL private val gson = Gson () @Throws ( Exception :: class ) fun main ( req : RuntimeRequest , res : RuntimeResponse ): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( req . payload . ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap :: class . java ) val number = payload [ "number" ] ?: 2 val url = URL ( "https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/$number" ) val con = ( url . openConnection () as HttpURLConnection ). apply { requestMethod = "GET" responseCode } val responseString = buildString { BufferedReader ( InputStreamReader ( con . inputStream )). useLines { lines -> lines . forEach { append ( it ) } } } con . disconnect () val response = gson . fromJson < Map < String , Any >>( responseString , MutableMap :: class . java ) return res . json ( response ) }

惊人的！ 我们都准备好测试我们的功能了！ 如果你还记得这个练习，我们需要做的就是部署我们的功能。

 appwrite deploy function ? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 ) ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json ✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

您现在可以前往 Appwrite 控制台并使用以下有效负载执行该函数。

转到“日志”选项卡以检查该功能的状态和响应。

完美的！ 看起来我们已经设法解决了数学中最紧迫的问题之一！ 更不用说，该 API 还带有许多古怪的广告！ 喜欢这个买老人👴

这使我们结束了本教程。 随意根据自己的喜好修改函数并使用 API。 如果您遇到困难或需要任何帮助，我们随时为您提供帮助。 只需前往我们Discord 服务器上的#support频道😊

这里有一些方便的链接以获取更多信息



原文:

One of the highlights of Appwrite’s latest release is the addition of four new Cloud Function runtimes! Java, Kotlin, .NET and C++ are now a part of our ever growing list of runtimes! 🤯

In this article, we’ll take a look at writing Cloud Functions using the Kotlin runtime. If you’re an Android developer, you could essentially build your app and write any necessary Cloud Functions without the need to learn any new language! 🤩

Appwrite is open source backend-as-a-service that abstracts all the complexity involved in building a modern application by providing you with a set of REST APIs for your core backend needs. Appwrite handles user authentication and authorization, realtime databases, file storage, cloud functions, webhooks and much more! If there is anything missing, you can extend Appwrite using your favorite backend language.

📝 Prerequisites

Before we get started, there are a couple of prerequisites. If you have the prerequisites setup already, you can skip to the following section.

In order to follow along, you’ll need a few things beforehand.

If you haven’t set up an Appwrite instance yet, you can follow the getting started guides to get up and running quickly. You can choose between One-click installations on DigitalOcean or Manual installations with Docker.

TL;DR - It's just a single command to install Appwrite

docker run -it --rm \ --volume /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock \ --volume "$(pwd)"/appwrite:/usr/src/code/appwrite:rw \ --entrypoint="install" \
    appwrite/appwrite:0.14.2

Once your server is up and running, head over to the Appwrite Dashboard on your server’s public IP address ( or localhost if you installed locally ) and create a new user account.

We’ll use the Appwrite CLI during this exercise as it makes the process super simple. If you have Node.js installed, the installation command is a simple

npm install -g appwrite-cli

If npm is not your thing, we have numerous installation options you can find in the getting started guide for the CLI.

🏁 Getting Started

With everything set up, we can now begin! Login to the Appwrite CLI using the following command and use the credentials we used when setting up the Appwrite server to login.

appwrite login
? Enter your email test@test.com
? Enter your password ********
✓ Success

Next, we need to create a new Appwrite project to work with. We can use the following command to set it up

appwrite init project
? How would you like to start? Create a new Appwrite project
? What would you like to name your project? My Awesome Project
✓ Success

You can give your project any name of your choice. I’m going to stick to the defaults in this case. You’ll notice a new appwrite.json file in the current directory which stores all the information about your project.

It’s time to start writing our function! But wait, we don't need to start from scratch! The CLI can setup all the boilerplate for us using the following command

 appwrite init function
? What would you like to name your function? kotlin-example
? What runtime would you like to use? Kotlin (kotlin-1.6)
✓ Success

Give your function a name and choose the Kotlin 1.6 runtime. This will create a new Appwrite Function in your project and set up all the boilerplate code necessary. Feel free to examine the files created in the functions/kotlin-example directory. You’ll find the CLI created a simple Kotlin function that returns a very important JSON message

{ "areDevelopersAwesome":true
}

Before we go ahead and modify the function, let’s deploy it to get a feel of the end-to-end workflow from initializing the function to deploying it.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example (62863924adaff7c88a10)
ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

The appwrite deploy function command packages your source code, uploads it to the Appwrite server and initiates the build process. During this time, all the function’s dependencies are installed and a .jar file is generated for your function.

You can head over to your Appwrite Dashboard to track the progress of your deployment.

Once the deployment completes, you can execute your function by clicking the Execute Now button.

The first execution results in what we call a Cold Start. Essentially this is the first time a runtime is created, so it takes a bit longer. As you can see, the first execution took about 450ms and subsequent executions were around 4ms. Now that we have an idea of the complete workflow, we can start tinkering with the code and write some cool functions.

🧮 Answering the Most Fundamental Question in Mathematics

As the title suggests we’re going to write a cloud function to answer one of the most fundamental questions in Mathematics.

Is the given number odd or even? I know what you’re thinking… It’s so easy! Why do I need a Cloud Function for it? I could just write

fun main() { val x = 12 println( x%2 == 0 ) // true println( (x/2) * 2 == x ) // true println( x and 1 == 0 ) // true println( ( x shr 1) shl 1 == x ) // true
}

But wait! We’re taking this to the next level. We’re going to decide if our number is even or odd using one of the most sophisticated and well written APIs out there! The isEven API.

Jokes aside, this example aims to illustrate how you can make API calls from your Cloud Function which will enable you to build anything you’d like.

Firstly, open the kotlin-example folder in your favorite IDE. Let’s start with a blank canvas and clean up our src/Index.kt file to

@Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { return res.json(mapOf( "message" to "Hello, World!" ))
}

In order to deal with JSON objects more easily, we’ll make use of the popular [gson](https://github.com/google/gson) library. Include the following dependency in the deps.gradle file.

dependencies { implementation 'com.google.code.gson:gson:2.9.0'
}

Next, let’s write some code to read and parse a number sent as an argument to this function.

import com.google.gson.Gson private val gson = Gson() @Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( req.payload.ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap::class.java ) val number = payload["number"] ?: 2 return res.json(mapOf( "message" to "Hello, World!" ))
}

Let’s go over the code we just wrote. From the functions documentation, we see that the payload is available through the request object and is a JSON string that needs to be converted to a JSON object to parse further. If the payload is empty, we replace it with an empty JSON object.

We then retrieve the number that was passed to the function and default to the number 2 in case it’s empty. We also import the gson library and create an instance of it.

Next, let’s make the call to the API using the native HTTP client.

import com.google.gson.Gson
import java.io.BufferedReader
import java.io.InputStreamReader
import java.net.HttpURLConnection
import java.net.URL private val gson = Gson() @Throws(Exception::class)
fun main(req: RuntimeRequest, res: RuntimeResponse): RuntimeResponse { val payload = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( req.payload.ifEmpty { "{}" }, MutableMap::class.java ) val number = payload["number"] ?: 2 val url = URL("https://api.isevenapi.xyz/api/iseven/$number") val con = (url.openConnection() as HttpURLConnection).apply { requestMethod = "GET" responseCode } val responseString = buildString { BufferedReader(InputStreamReader(con.inputStream)).useLines { lines -> lines.forEach { append(it) } } } con.disconnect() val response = gson.fromJson<Map<String, Any>>( responseString, MutableMap::class.java ) return res.json(response)
}

Awesome! We’re all set to test our function! If you remember the drill, all we need to do is deploy our function.

appwrite deploy function
? Which functions would you like to deploy? kotlin-example (62863924adaff7c88a10)
ℹ Info Deploying function kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )
ℹ Info Ignoring files using configuration from appwrite.json
✓ Success Deployed kotlin-example ( 62863924adaff7c88a10 )

You can now head over to the Appwrite console and execute the function with the following payload.

Head over to the Logs tab to check the status and response from the function.

Perfect! Looks like we’ve managed to solve one of the most pressing problems in Mathematics after all! Not to mention, the API also comes with a host of quirky Ads! Like this one to purchase old people 👴

And that brings us to the end of this tutorial. Feel free to modify the function to your liking and play around with the API. If you’re stuck or need any help, we’re always here to help. Simply head over to the #support channels on our Discord Server 😊

📚 Resources

Here are some handy links for more information

Items.Title: 让我们使用 React.js 和 Tailwind CSS 构建社交证明部分。 | Let's build Social Proof Section using React.js & Tailwind CSS.
Items.Description:
大家好，我希望你们都做得很好。 我带着另一个令人兴奋的 Web 开发项目回来了，这将有助于作为前端开发人员学习一些新的 Web 开发技能。

在本教程中，我将向您展示如何构建网站的社交证明部分。 这是前端导师挑战项目之一，我们的目标是让它看起来像前端导师给出的设计。

这是我们将构建的FrontendMentor挑战的链接。

所以没有任何进一步的讨论，让我们开始吧🚀。

我想提一件事，这个项目是关于设计的，没有任何 React.js 钩子或任何编程的东西。

我们将使用 Tailwind CSS 作为 CSS 框架来设计我们的 Web 应用程序。

创建一个新的 next-js 应用，其中包含引导式 Tailwind CSS。

您可以使用这个单行命令创建一个带有 TypeScript 和 Tailwind CSS 的新 Next.js 应用程序。

 npx create-next-app -e with-tailwindcss my-project-name

您可以随意命名您的项目，我将其命名为social-proof-section。

现在创建项目后，在 Vs Code 或您喜欢的任何 IDE 中打开它。

在pages目录中找到index.tsx文件并删除所有内容并粘贴下面的给定代码。

 import type { NextPage } from ' next ' import Head from ' next/head ' const Home : NextPage = () => { return ( < div className = "flex min-h-screen flex-col items-center bg-[#1e1f29]" > < Head > < title > Social Proof Section </ title > < link rel = "icon" href = "/favicon.ico" /> </ Head > )} export default Home;

之后进入第二步。

步骤 2 创建组件

现在，是时候创建一些我们将在应用程序中使用的组件了。

如果您访问FrontendMentor挑战链接，您将看到标题文本、开始评分和推荐卡三种不同类型的组件。

在目录的根目录下创建一个新的文件夹名称components ，并在其中创建 5 个文件。

  1. 页眉文本.tsx
  2. 星星.tsx
  3. 推荐书.tsx

创建文件后，我们将开始将代码一一添加到每个文件中。

该组件由放置在 Div 标签内的H1 标签和P 标签组成。

 import React from ' react ' const SUB_TEXT = " We only provide great products with excellent customer service. See what our satisfied customer are saying about our services. " ; const HEAD_TEXT = " 10,000+ of our users loves our products. " ; export const HeaderText = () => { return ( < div className = "group flex flex-col w-3/4 md:w-1/3 space-y-6 md:space-y-8 px-6 py-6 rounded-lg justify-start mt-6" > < h1 className = "text-3xl md:w-[360px] md:text-5xl text-fuchsia-800 font-bold" > { HEAD_TEXT } </ h1 > < p className = "text-md md:text-lg text-purple-900 font-medium" > { SUB_TEXT } </ p > </ div > ) }

该组件包含一个 Star 的SVG ，它将在 StarRating.tsx 组件中使用。

 import React from ' react ' export const Star = () => { return ( < svg xmlns = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className = " h-5 w-5 text-fuchsia-500 group-hover:text-fuchsia-100" viewBox = "0 0 20 20" fill = "currentColor" > < path d = "M9.049 2.927c.3-.921 1.603-.921 1.902 0l1.07 3.292a1 1 0 00.95.69h3.462c.969 0 1.371 1.24.588 1.81l-2.8 2.034a1 1 0 00-.364 1.118l1.07 3.292c.3.921-.755 1.688-1.54 1.118l-2.8-2.034a1 1 0 00-1.175 0l-2.8 2.034c-.784.57-1.838-.197-1.539-1.118l1.07-3.292a1 1 0 00-.364-1.118L2.98 8.72c-.783-.57-.38-1.81.588-1.81h3.461a1 1 0 00.951-.69l1.07-3.292z" /> </ svg > ) }

它由5 个 Star.tsx 组件和一个P 标签组成，该标签放置在一个 Div 标签内，并被制成为Flex-row方向。

 import React from ' react ' import { Star } from ' ./Star ' interface Prop { text : string , } export const StarRating = ({ text } : Prop ) => { return ( < div className = "w-[260px] md:w-[350px] group flex flex-col space-y-2 py-2 md:flex-row items-center md:justify-start md:space-y-0 md:space-x-4 my-4 px-4 md:py-3 bg-fuchsia-300 rounded-md cursor-pointer hover:bg-fuchsia-500 transition duration-300 ease-in shadow-md hover:shadow-xl md:even:ml-16 md:last:ml-32" > < div className = "flex space-x-1" > < Star /> < Star /> < Star /> < Star /> < Star /> </ div > < p className = "text-sm flex justify-start items-center md:text-md font-semibold text-fuchsia-700 group-hover:text-fuchsia-200 " > { text } </ p > </ div > ) }

它由一个Image Tag、H2 Tag、P tag 和 Div Tag组成，它是一个简单的卡片组件，具有一些良好的设计和悬停动画。

 import Image from ' next/image ' import React from ' react ' interface TesimonialProps { customerName : string , testimonialText : string , imageURL : string , } export const TestimonialCard = ({ customerName , testimonialText , imageURL }: TesimonialProps ) => { return ( < div className = { `group w-3/4 md:w-[420px] md:h-[240px] flex flex-col items-start md:space-x-4 bg-gradient-to-r from-[#975297] to-[#502050] mt-4 md:even:mt-16 md:last:mt-32 px-6 py-6 rounded-2xl cursor-pointer hover:shadow-md hover:bg-gradient-to-r hover:from-[#ce74ff] hover:to-[#743c8a] transition duration-1000 ease-in-out ` } > < div className = "flex space-x-5 pb-4 md:space-x-6 md:px-4 " > < div className = "w-[50px] h-[50px] rounded-full ring-4 ring-fuchsia-400 group-hover:ring-fuchsia-100" > < Image src = { imageURL } objectFit = "contain" width = { 50 } height = { 50 } className = "rounded-full" /> </ div > < div className = " flex flex-col items-start justify-start" > < h2 className = "text-md md:text-2xl font-bold text-fuchsia-400 group-hover:text-fuchsia-100 " > { customerName } </ h2 > < p className = "text-xs md:text-md font-extralight text-gray-100 ml-1 group-hover:text-fuchsia-50" > Verified Buyer </ p > </ div > </ div > < div className = "text-md md:text-lg text-fuchsia-200 font-500 py-2" > { testimonialText } </ div > </ div > ) }

卡片的背景是渐变背景。

它由 3 个TestimonialCard.tsx组件组成，这些组件位于Flex 行方向的Div 标签内。

 import React from ' react ' import { TestimonialCard } from ' ./TestimonialCard ' const TESTIMONIAL_DATA = [ { customerName : " Colton Smith " , imageURL : " /image-colton.jpg " , testimonialText : " We needed the same printed design as the one we had ordered a week prior.Not only did they find the original order, but we also received it in time.Excellent! " , }, { customerName : " Irene Roberts " , imageURL : " /image-irene.jpg " , testimonialText : " Customer service is always excellent and very quick turn around. Completely delighted with the simplicity of the purchase and the speed of delivery. " , }, { customerName : " Anne Wallace " , imageURL : " /image-anne.jpg " , testimonialText : " Put an order with this company and can only praise them for the very high standard. Will definitely use them again and recommend them to everyone! " , }, ]; export const Testimonials = () => { return ( < div className = " flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-center items-center gap-10 " > { TESTIMONIAL_DATA . map (( item ) => { return < TestimonialCard customerName = { item . customerName } imageURL = { item . imageURL } testimonialText = { item . testimonialText } / > }) } < /div > ) }

正如您在FrontendMentor挑战设计中看到的那样，这里的主要挑战是StarRating.tsx和TestimonialCard.tsx组件发生了变化。

在StarRating.tsx 组件的情况下，第二个和第三个有一些左边距，在 TestimonialCard.tsx 组件的情况下，它们有一些上边距。

因此，为了实现这些设计，我们必须在 StartRating.tsx 组件中添加两个特殊类even:ml-16 和 last:ml-32 **，在 TestimonialCard.tsx 组件中添加 **even:mt-16 和 last:mt-32 。

现在是时候导入我们在 Pages 文件夹内的 index.tsx 文件中创建的组件了。

内部 Index.tsx 页面

import type { NextPage } from ' next ' import Head from ' next/head ' import { HeaderText } from ' ../components/HeaderText ' ; import { StarRating } from ' ../components/StarRating ' import { Testimonials } from ' ../components/Testimonials ' ; const Home : NextPage = () => { return ( < div className = "flex min-h-screen background flex-col bg-gray-100 items-center " > < Head > < title > Social Proof Section </ title > < link rel = "icon" href = "/favicon.ico" /> </ Head > < div className = "flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center justify-between mx-a px-4 md:px-24 py-2 " > < HeaderText /> < div className = "flex flex-col justify-center items-center" > < StarRating text = "Rated 5 Stars in Reviews" /> < StarRating text = "Rated 5 Stars in Report Guru" /> < StarRating text = "Rated 5 Stars in Best Tech" /> </ div > </ div > < Testimonials /> </ div > ) } export default Home

结论

希望你能够建立这个社会证明部分。 如果您喜欢这个项目，请随时在Twitter 上关注我并分享此项目😉。

我希望你喜欢这个项目并喜欢构建它，如果你能分享这篇博文，我将不胜感激✌️。

如果您认为这很有帮助，那么请考虑访问我的博客网站nextjsdev.com并在Twitter 上关注我并在LinkedIn上与我联系。

如果你被困在某个地方并且无法找到解决方案，你可以在这里查看我完成的Github Repo 。

感谢您花时间阅读这个项目，如果您喜欢这个项目，请在 Twitter 和 Facebook 或任何其他社交媒体上分享并标记我。

我会在我的下一篇博客中见到你✌️。 在此之前，请注意并继续建设项目。

一些有用的链接：

项目的 Github 链接

联系我：

推特简介

领英简介

脸书个人资料



原文:

Hello everyone, I hope you all are doing well. I am back with another exciting web dev project, which will help to learn some new web dev skills as a Front-end developer.

In this tutorial, I will show you how you can build a Social Proof Section of a website. It is one of the Frontend Mentor challenge projects and our goal is to make it look like the design given by the Frontend Mentor.

Here is the link to the FrontendMentor challenge that we will build.

So without any further talk, Let's get started 🚀.

🚀 Live Demo of the Project

I want to mention one thing, this project is all about designing there is no use for any React.js hooks or any programming thing.

We will be using Tailwind CSS, as a CSS framework to design our web application.

💡 We are using TypeScript in this Project, so we have to explicitly define all the props, constants & function data types.

Step-1 Create the starting project

Create a new next-js app with Tailwind CSS bootstrapped in it.

You can use this one-line command to create a new Next.js app with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS.

npx create-next-app -e with-tailwindcss my-project-name

You can name your project whatever you want, I will name it as social-proof-section.

Now after creating the project open it in Vs Code or any IDE that you prefer.

Find the index.tsx file inside pages directory and delete everything and paste the given code below.

import type { NextPage } from 'next'
import Head from 'next/head' const Home: NextPage = () => { return ( <div className="flex min-h-screen flex-col items-center bg-[#1e1f29]"> <Head> <title>Social Proof Section</title> <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" /> </Head>

)}

export default Home;

After that move to the second step.

Step-2 Creating the Components

Now, it is time to create some components that we are going to use in the app.

If you visit the FrontendMentor challenge link, you will see that there are three different types of components Heading Text, Start Rating, and the Testimonial Card.

Create a new folder name components in the root of the directory, and create 5 files inside it.

  1. HeaderText.tsx
  2. Star.tsx
  3. StarRating.tsx
  4. TestimonialCard.tsx
  5. Testimonials.tsx

After creating the file we will start adding the code to each file one by one.

The HeaderText.tsx Component

This component consists of an H1 tag and a P tag which are placed inside a Div tag.

import React from 'react' const SUB_TEXT="We only provide great products with excellent customer service. See what our satisfied customer are saying about our services."; const HEAD_TEXT="10,000+ of our users loves our products."; export const HeaderText = () => { return ( <div className="group flex flex-col w-3/4 md:w-1/3 space-y-6 md:space-y-8 px-6 py-6 rounded-lg justify-start mt-6"> <h1 className="text-3xl md:w-[360px] md:text-5xl text-fuchsia-800 font-bold">{HEAD_TEXT}</h1> <p className="text-md md:text-lg text-purple-900 font-medium">{SUB_TEXT}</p> </div> )
}

The Star.tsx Component

This component consists of an SVG of a Star which will be used inside the StarRating.tsx Component.

import React from 'react' export const Star = () => { return ( <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" className=" h-5 w-5 text-fuchsia-500 group-hover:text-fuchsia-100" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor"> <path d="M9.049 2.927c.3-.921 1.603-.921 1.902 0l1.07 3.292a1 1 0 00.95.69h3.462c.969 0 1.371 1.24.588 1.81l-2.8 2.034a1 1 0 00-.364 1.118l1.07 3.292c.3.921-.755 1.688-1.54 1.118l-2.8-2.034a1 1 0 00-1.175 0l-2.8 2.034c-.784.57-1.838-.197-1.539-1.118l1.07-3.292a1 1 0 00-.364-1.118L2.98 8.72c-.783-.57-.38-1.81.588-1.81h3.461a1 1 0 00.951-.69l1.07-3.292z" /> </svg> )
}

The StarRating.tsx Component

It consists of 5 Star.tsx Component and a P tag which is placed inside a Div Tag and is made to be in Flex-row direction.

import React from 'react'
import { Star } from './Star' interface Prop{ text:string,
} export const StarRating = ({text} :Prop) => { return ( <div className="w-[260px] md:w-[350px] group flex flex-col space-y-2 py-2 md:flex-row items-center md:justify-start md:space-y-0 md:space-x-4 my-4 px-4 md:py-3 bg-fuchsia-300 rounded-md cursor-pointer hover:bg-fuchsia-500 transition duration-300 ease-in shadow-md hover:shadow-xl md:even:ml-16 md:last:ml-32"> <div className="flex space-x-1"> <Star /> <Star /> <Star /> <Star /> <Star /> </div> <p className="text-sm flex justify-start items-center md:text-md font-semibold text-fuchsia-700 group-hover:text-fuchsia-200 ">{text}</p> </div> )
}

The TestimonialCard.tsx Component

It consists of an Image Tag, H2 Tag, P tag, and Div Tag , It is a simple Card Component with some good design and hover animations.

import Image from 'next/image'
import React from 'react' interface TesimonialProps { customerName: string, testimonialText: string, imageURL: string,
} export const TestimonialCard = ({ customerName, testimonialText, imageURL }: TesimonialProps) => { return ( <div className={`group w-3/4 md:w-[420px] md:h-[240px] flex flex-col items-start md:space-x-4 bg-gradient-to-r from-[#975297] to-[#502050] mt-4 md:even:mt-16 md:last:mt-32 px-6 py-6 rounded-2xl cursor-pointer hover:shadow-md hover:bg-gradient-to-r hover:from-[#ce74ff] hover:to-[#743c8a] transition duration-1000 ease-in-out `}> <div className="flex space-x-5 pb-4 md:space-x-6 md:px-4 "> <div className="w-[50px] h-[50px] rounded-full ring-4 ring-fuchsia-400 group-hover:ring-fuchsia-100"> <Image src={imageURL} objectFit="contain" width={50} height={50} className="rounded-full" /> </div> <div className=" flex flex-col items-start justify-start"> <h2 className="text-md md:text-2xl font-bold text-fuchsia-400 group-hover:text-fuchsia-100 ">{customerName}</h2> <p className="text-xs md:text-md font-extralight text-gray-100 ml-1 group-hover:text-fuchsia-50">Verified Buyer</p> </div> </div> <div className="text-md md:text-lg text-fuchsia-200 font-500 py-2"> {testimonialText} </div> </div> )
}

The background of the Card is a gradient background.

The Testimonials.tsx Component

It consists of 3 TestimonialCard.tsx Components which are placed inside a Div Tag in the Flex-row direction.

import React from 'react'
import { TestimonialCard } from './TestimonialCard' const TESTIMONIAL_DATA = [ { customerName: "Colton Smith", imageURL: "/image-colton.jpg", testimonialText: "We needed the same printed design as the one we had ordered a week prior.Not only did they find the original order, but we also received it in time.Excellent!", }, { customerName: "Irene Roberts", imageURL: "/image-irene.jpg", testimonialText: "Customer service is always excellent and very quick turn around. Completely delighted with the simplicity of the purchase and the speed of delivery.", }, { customerName: "Anne Wallace", imageURL: "/image-anne.jpg", testimonialText: "Put an order with this company and can only praise them for the very high standard. Will definitely use them again and recommend them to everyone!", }, ]; export const Testimonials = () => { return ( <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row justify-center items-center gap-10"> { TESTIMONIAL_DATA.map((item) => { return <TestimonialCard customerName={item.customerName} imageURL={item.imageURL} testimonialText={item.testimonialText} />
 }) } </div>
 )
}

The main challenge here is as you can see in the FrontendMentor challenge design, the StarRating.tsx and TestimonialCard.tsx component are shifted.

In the Case of StarRating.tsx Component, the 2nd and 3rd have some Left Margin and in the case of the TestimonialCard.tsx Component, they have some Top Margin.

So to achieve these design we have to add two special classes even:ml-16 and last:ml-32 ** in StartRating.tsx Component and **even:mt-16 and last:mt-32 in TestimonialCard.tsx Component.

💡 What these two classes are doing is that every even position children element inside the div or a section will have a margin-top or margin-left equal to 16px, and every last child inside the div or a section will have a margin-top or margin-left equal to 32px.

Step-3 Import the Components

Now it's time to import the component that we have created inside the index.tsx file inside the Pages folder.

Inside Index.tsx Page

import type { NextPage } from 'next'
import Head from 'next/head'
import { HeaderText } from '../components/HeaderText';
import { StarRating } from '../components/StarRating'
import { Testimonials } from '../components/Testimonials'; const Home: NextPage = () => { return ( <div className="flex min-h-screen background flex-col bg-gray-100 items-center "> <Head> <title>Social Proof Section</title> <link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico" /> </Head> <div className="flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center justify-between mx-a px-4 md:px-24 py-2 "> <HeaderText /> <div className="flex flex-col justify-center items-center"> <StarRating text="Rated 5 Stars in Reviews" /> <StarRating text="Rated 5 Stars in Report Guru" /> <StarRating text="Rated 5 Stars in Best Tech" /> </div> </div> <Testimonials /> </div> )
} export default Home

Conclusion

Hope you were able to build this Social Proof Section. Feel free to follow me on Twitter and share this if you like this project 😉.

I hope you like this project and enjoyed building it, I would appreciate ✌️ it if you could share this blog post.

If you think that this was helpful and then please do consider visiting my blog website nextjsdev.com and do follow me on Twitter and connect with me on LinkedIn.

If you were stuck somewhere and not able to find the solution you can check out my completed Github Repo here.

Thanks for your time to read this project, if you like this please share it on Twitter and Facebook or any other social media and tag me there.

I will see you in my next blog ✌️. Till then take care and keep building projects.

Some Useful Link:

Next.js and Tailwind Installation Docs

Github link for the project

Connect with me:

Twitter Profile

LinkedIn Profile

GitHub Profile

Facebook Profile
Items.Author: raivikas

Items.Title: 在 Debian Linux 上使用 Visual Studio Code 的简单开发环境 | A Simple Dev Environment with Visual Studio Code on Debian Linux
Items.Description:
长期以来，在 Linux 中使用 Visual Studio 一直是我的个人目标，虽然 Visual Studio 和 Visual Studio Code (VS Code) 的 Linux 版本已经推出了很长一段时间，但我最近才能够设置我想要的东西在 Linux 上为我自己考虑一个可用的、简单的开发环境。 经过几天的使用，我对结果非常满意，并想分享我设置环境和工具所采取的步骤。

这篇简短的文章将详细介绍为编辑和上传网页等基本 Web 开发任务设置三个工具； 这是我最常使用的三个工具，在我真正致力于使用操作系统进行日常开发之前，它们都需要在 Linux 上完美运行。

介绍

要开始使用，您需要一台 Debian Linux 机器。 如果您没有可用的 Linux 机器，您可以下载 Debian ISO并安装 VirtualBox以创建虚拟机。

安装 VS 代码

首先，通过选择 .deb 文件下载 VSCode 。 如果您无法找到 .deb 文件（或者您没有从 Linux 机器访问该页面），请查看其他 VSCode 平台部分以获取其他选项。

下载 .deb 文件后，导航到该位置并执行以下命令（将 code_1.67.0-1651667246_amd64 替换为正确的文件名）：

 sudo apt install ./code_1.67.0-1651667246_amd64.deb

如果安装成功，VS Code 将出现在 Applications Menu 的 Applications > Development 下。

有关详细信息，请访问在 Linux 上运行 Visual Studio 。

安装 GitHub

下载 .deb 文件后，按照与安装 VS Code 相同的步骤进行操作——导航到文件的位置并执行以下命令（将 GitHubDesktop-linux-3.0.0-linux1 替换为正确的文件名）：

 sudo apt install ./GitHubDesktop-linux-3.0.0-linux1.deb

如果安装成功，GitHub Desktop 将出现在 Applications Menu 的 Applications > Accessories 下。

有关更多信息或有关如何从命令行安装 GitHub Desktop 的说明，请查看这篇精彩的文章： GitHub Desktop 是否可用于 GNU/Linux？

安装超越比较

虽然该软件不是免费的，但有 30 天的试用期。 我已经使用 Beyond Compare 很多年了，并且相信这是物有所值的，因为单个个人许可证可以在多个机器和平台上使用。

要开始使用，请下载 Beyond Compare 的 .deb 包。 然后，再次导航到文件的位置并执行以下命令（将 bcompare-4.4.2.26348_amd64 替换为正确的文件名）：

 sudo apt install ./bcompare-4.4.2.26348_amd64.deb

如果安装成功，Beyond Compare 将出现在 Applications Menu 的 Applications > Development 下。

如需更多信息，尤其是如果您使用的是旧版本的 Debian，请访问Beyond Compare：Linux 安装说明。

结论

Debian Linux is a fast, free GNU/Linux distribution and spinning up a Debian virtual machine for basic web development tasks like editing source code, tracking modifications, and uploading changes, can be accomplished fairly quickly and painlessly with many popular tools and services offering Linux packages. Debian Linux是一个快速、免费的 GNU/Linux 发行版，它为基本的 Web 开发任务（如编辑源代码、跟踪修改和上传更改）启动 Debian 虚拟机，可以使用许多提供 Linux 的流行工具和服务相当快速、轻松地完成包。



原文:

Using Visual Studio with Linux has long been a personal goal of mine, and while Linux versions of both Visual Studio and Visual Studio Code (VS Code) have been available for quite some time, I've only recently been able to setup what I would consider a useable, simple dev environment for myself on Linux. After several days of use, I'm pretty happy with the results and wanted to share the steps I took to setup my environment and tools.

This brief post will detail setting up three tools for basic web development tasks like editing and uploading webpages; 1) an IDE (VS Code), 2) a source control (GitHub Desktop), and 3) a file comparison tool (Beyond Compare). These are the three tools I use most heavily, and needed each to work perfectly on Linux before I could really commit to using the OS for daily development.

Introduction

To get started you will need a Debian Linux machine. If you do not have a Linux machine available you can download a Debian ISO, and install VirtualBox to create a virtual machine.

Installing VS Code

Visual Studio Code or VS Code is a source code editor with support for a wide variety of languages and is available for multiple platforms including Windows, macOS, and Linux. To get started, download VSCode by selecting the .deb file. If you have trouble finding the .deb file (or you are not accessing the page from a Linux machine), check out the other VSCode platforms section for additional options.

After downloading the .deb file, navigate to the location and execute the following command (replacing code_1.67.0-1651667246_amd64 with the correct file name):

sudo apt install ./code_1.67.0-1651667246_amd64.deb

If the installation was successful, VS Code will appear under Applications > Development in the Applications Menu.

For more information, visit Running Visual Studio on Linux.

Installing GitHub

GitHub is a popular code repository hosting service, and provides source control through a Web-based interface or desktop application. GitHub Desktop for Linux is not available from the app's primary download page, but it is available under the Assets section of each release.

After downloading the .deb file, follow the same steps used to install VS Code — navigate to the file's location and execute the following command (replacing GitHubDesktop-linux-3.0.0-linux1 with the correct file name):

sudo apt install ./GitHubDesktop-linux-3.0.0-linux1.deb

If the installation was successful, GitHub Desktop will appear under Applications > Accessories in the Applications Menu.

For more information, or for instructions on how to install GitHub Desktop from the command line, check out this great post: Is GitHub Desktop available for GNU/Linux?

Installing Beyond Compare

Beyond Compare is an excellent file comparison utility from Scooter Software, and can also be used as an FTP client. While the software is not free, there is a 30-day trial available. I've used Beyond Compare for many years, and believe it is well worth the cost, as a single, personal license can be used on multiple machines and platforms.

To get started, download the .deb package for Beyond Compare. Then, once again, navigate to the file's location and execute the following command (replacing bcompare-4.4.2.26348_amd64 with the correct file name):

sudo apt install ./bcompare-4.4.2.26348_amd64.deb

If the installation was successful, Beyond Compare will appear under Applications > Development in the Applications Menu.

For more information, especially if you are using an older version of Debian, visit Beyond Compare: Linux Installation Instructions.

Conclusion

Debian Linux is a fast, free GNU/Linux distribution and spinning up a Debian virtual machine for basic web development tasks like editing source code, tracking modifications, and uploading changes, can be accomplished fairly quickly and painlessly with many popular tools and services offering Linux packages.

Items.Title: 让我们说一下搜索引擎优化。 | Let's say about Search engine optimization.
Items.Description:
为什么SEO很重要？

搜索引擎优化很重要，因为它使您的网站更加可见，这意味着更多的流量和更多将潜在客户转化为客户的机会。

搜索引擎优化基础

找到以最佳方式满足他们需求的结果在 SEO 中非常重要。
您需要创建高质量、有意图和新鲜的内容

  • 关键词研究与选择

关键字决定了您如何建立链接，包括从您选择的策略到您计划如何实施它们的所有内容。

您网站的 HTML 是 SEO 营销难题的重要组成部分。
您需要很好地实现标题标签、元描述、架构、副标题、替代文本、URL Slug

良好的网站架构会带来出色的用户体验，这对于 SEO 营销很重要

如果您的网站加载太慢或某些元素加载缓慢，Google 可能会惩罚您或让您更难超越竞争对手。

安全和安全是重要的排名因素。 如果 Google 认为您的网站是垃圾邮件或粗略，它不会为您提供首页排名。



原文:

SEO stands for Search Engine Optimization and is the process used to optimize a website's technical configuration, content relevance and link popularity so its pages can become easily findable, more relevant and popular towards user search queries , and as a consequence, search engines rank them better.

Why is SEO important?

SEO is important because it makes your website more visible, and that means more traffic and more opportunities to convert prospects into customers.

SEO Basics

Finding the result that serves their needs in the best way is very important in SEO.
You need to create high-quality, intent and freshness content

  • Keyword Research and Selection

Keywords determine how you build links, including everything from the tactics you choose to how you plan on implementing them.

Your site's HTML is an important piece of the SEO marketing puzzle.
You need to implement Title tags, Meta Description, Schema, Subheadings, Alt Text, URL Slug well

A good website architecture leads to a great user experience, which is important for SEO marketing

If your site loads too slow or certain elements load slowly, Google may penalize you or make it more difficult for you to outrank your competition.

Security and safety are the important ranking factors. If Google thinks your site is spammy or sketchy, it’s not going to give you a first-page ranking.

Items.Title: Como testes ajudam a melhorar o design do código? | Como testes ajudam a melhorar o design do código?
Items.Description:
麦克苏尔·费尔南德斯·马卡里

发表于副作用

标志 criei o 行为Log :

 defmodule Q3Reporter . Log do @moduledoc false alias Q3Reporter . Log . FileAdapter @type read_return :: { :ok , String . t ()} | { :error , atom ()} @type mtime_return :: { :ok , NaiveDateTime . t ()} | { :error , atom ()} @callback read ( String . t ()) :: read_return @callback mtime ( String . t ()) :: mtime_return @default_adapter Application . compile_env ( :q3_reporter , :log_adapter , FileAdapter ) @spec read ( String . t (), atom () | nil ) :: read_return () def ( read ( path , adapter \\ @default_adapter )) def read ( path , adapter ) when is_nil ( adapter ), do : @default_adapter . read ( path ) def read ( path , adapter ), do : adapter . read ( path ) @spec mtime ( String . t (), atom () | nil ) :: mtime_return () def mtime ( path , adapter \\ @default_adapter ) def mtime ( path , adapter ) when is_nil ( adapter ), do : @default_adapter . mtime ( path ) def mtime ( path , adapter ), do : adapter . mtime ( path ) end

徽标，eu nãodependia mais de criar e manipular arquivos do sistema。 E caso precisasse realizar testes que liam arquivos reais, eu apenas tinha de passar o Log.FileAdapter para a função Q3Reporter.parse/2 : E caso precisasse realizar testes que liam arquivos reais, eu apenas tinha de passar o Log.FileAdapter para a função Q3Reporter.parse/2 :

 test "should return game contents with valid file and default mode" do assert { :ok , results } = Q3Reporter . parse ( @path , mode: :by_game , log_adapter: FileAdapter ) assert % Results { entries: [%{}], mode: :by_game } = results end

Cobertura de Testes Cobertura de Testes

Enfrentei outro problema ao tentar testar erros relacionados a File.read/1 , como "falta de memória" e "permissões". Enfrentei outro questiona aotentar testar erros relacionados a File.read/1 , como "falta de memória" e "permissões"。 Porém não é viável eu manipular arquivos os quais eu não tenho permissões no projeto, e nem lotar a memória do meu computador enquanto os testes eram realizados. Porém não é viável eu manipular arquivos os quais eu não tenho permissões no projeto, e nem lotar a memória do meu computador enquanto os testes eram realizados。

A minha primeira solução, no caso, foi ignorar esses trechos de código na cobertura de testes: A minha primeira solução, no caso, foi ignorar esses trechos de código na cobertura de testes：

 def parse ( path , opts \\ []) do with { :ok , content } <- Log . read ( path , opts [ :log_adapter ]), results <- Core . log_to_results ( content , opts [ :mode ]) do { :ok , results } else { :error , :enoent } -> { :error , "' #{ path } ' not found..." } # coveralls-ignore-start { :error , :eacces } -> { :error , "You don't have permission to open ' #{ path } ..." } { :error , :enomem } -> { :error , "There's no enough memory to open 'invalid'..." } { :error , _ } -> { :error , "Error trying to open 'invalid'" } # coveralls-ignore-stop end end

Como a refatoração da sessão anterior me permitiu substituir o adapter, logo eu poderia testar esse trecho da seguinte maneira: Como a refatoração da sessão anterior me permitiu substituir o adapter, logo eu poderia testar esse trecho da seguinte maneira:

 test "should return error with invalid file path" do assert { :error , "'invalid' not found..." } = Q3Reporter . parse ( "invalid" , log_adapter: FileAdapter ) assert { :error , "You don't have permission to open 'invalid'..." } = Q3Reporter . parse ( "invalid" , log_adapter: error_adapter ( :eacces )) assert { :error , "There's no enough memory to open 'invalid'..." } = Q3Reporter . parse ( "invalid" , log_adapter: error_adapter ( :enomem )) assert { :error , "Error trying to open 'invalid'" } = Q3Reporter . parse ( "invalid" , log_adapter: error_adapter ( :unknown )) end

O error_adapter/1 não é nada mais do que uma função que utiliza o Mox para mockar um adapter, e retornar o erro esperado: O error_adapter/1 não é nada mais do que uma função que utiliza o Mox para mockar um adapter, e retornar o erro esperado:

 def error_adapter ( error ) do module = module_name ( error ) Mox . defmock ( module , for: Q3Reporter . Log ) module |> expect ( :read , fn _ -> { :error , error } end ) |> expect ( :mtime , fn _ -> { :error , error } end ) end

Logo, substituir o File por um contrato explícito através de Log me permitiu atingir uma cobertura de testes onde não era possível anteriormente, e eu pude tirar todos os #coveralls-ignore do projeto e ter 100% de cobertura de testes real. Logo, substituir o File por um contrato explícito através de Log me permitiu atingir uma cobertura de testes onde não era possível anteriormente, e eu pude tirar todos os #coveralls-ignore do projeto e ter 100% de cobertura de testes real。

É possível conferir o código de exemplo desse projeto em: https://github.com/maxmaccari/q3_reporter é possível conferir o código de exemplo desse projeto em: https://github.com/maxmaccari/q3_reporter

Mangabo Kolawole - Mangabo Kolawole - Apr 17 4月17日

Diego (Relatable Code) - Diego（相关代码）- Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

Maxsuel Fernandes Maccari

Posted on May 21

Recentemente eu estive trabalhando numa aplicação somente para praticar Elixir. E ao testar a aplicação, eu enfrentei diversos problemas que me ajudaram a deixar a aplicação mais extensível e legível.

Side Effects

Um dos principais problemas que eu enfrentei escrevendo os testes foi de que o código que eu precisava testar dependia de arquivos do sistema:

with {:ok, content} <- File.read(path), results <- Core.log_to_results(content, opts[:mode]) do {:ok, results}
end

Lidar com esse tipo de efeito colateral uma única vez não me causou nenhum problema, principalmente quando se tratava apenas de leitura de arquivos.

Porém, ao implementar um processo que notificava as mudanças nos arquivos de logs, acabei escrevendo testes que criavam arquivos temporários, como na imagem abaixo:

Então enfrentei dois problemas com essa abordagem:

  • Não pude mais criar testes assíncronos, pois esses arquivos eram criados e manipulados no sistema;
  • Caso os testes falhassem, os arquivos temporários ainda continuavam existindo no file system, e eu tinha que apagá-los manualmente.

Há muito tempo eu li um post do José Valim sobre Mocks and explicit contracts, e isso me inspirou a refatorar o projeto para não depender mais de File.read/1 e File.stats/1. Logo criei o behaviour Log:

defmodule Q3Reporter.Log do @moduledoc false alias Q3Reporter.Log.FileAdapter @type read_return :: {:ok, String.t()} | {:error, atom()} @type mtime_return :: {:ok, NaiveDateTime.t()} | {:error, atom()} @callback read(String.t()) :: read_return @callback mtime(String.t()) :: mtime_return @default_adapter Application.compile_env(:q3_reporter, :log_adapter, FileAdapter) @spec read(String.t(), atom() | nil) :: read_return() def(read(path, adapter \\ @default_adapter)) def read(path, adapter) when is_nil(adapter), do: @default_adapter.read(path) def read(path, adapter), do: adapter.read(path) @spec mtime(String.t(), atom() | nil) :: mtime_return() def mtime(path, adapter \\ @default_adapter) def mtime(path, adapter) when is_nil(adapter), do: @default_adapter.mtime(path) def mtime(path, adapter), do: adapter.mtime(path)
end

A vantagem dessa abordagem é de que eu não precisava mais saber como a leitura dos logs era feita. Era necessário apenas respeitar o contrato e receber {:ok, conteúdo} para o read/1. E também não precisei mais tratar o resultado do File.stats/1, já que o contrato de mtime/1, que era {:ok, novo_mtime}, já trazia o dado que eu precisava. Logo, a implementação do Log.FileAdapter ficou:

defmodule Q3Reporter.Log.FileAdapter do @behaviour Q3Reporter.Log @impl true def read(path), do: File.read(path) @impl true def mtime(path) do case File.stat(path) do {:ok, stat} -> NaiveDateTime.from_erl(stat.mtime) {:error, _} = error -> error end end
end

Então eu pude criar uma implementação que simplesmente manipulava os "arquivos" na memória utilizando ETS:

defmodule Q3Reporter.Log.ETSAdapter do @behaviour Q3Reporter.Log @table __MODULE__ @impl true def read(name) do case :ets.lookup(@table, name) do [{_name, content, _mtime}] -> {:ok, content} _ -> {:error, :enoent} end end @impl true def mtime(name) do case :ets.lookup(@table, name) do [{_name, _content, mtime}] -> {:ok, mtime} _ -> {:error, :enoent} end end @doc false def init, do: :ets.new(@table, [:named_table, :set, :public]) @doc false def close(name), do: :ets.delete(@table, name) @doc false def push(name, content \\ "", mtime \\ NaiveDateTime.utc_now()) do :ets.insert(@table, {name, content, mtime}) end
end

E configurar meus testes para utilizar esse adapter por padrão em tests.exs:

config :q3_reporter, log_adapter: Q3Reporter.Log.ETSAdapter

Logo, eu não dependia mais de criar e manipular arquivos do sistema. E caso precisasse realizar testes que liam arquivos reais, eu apenas tinha de passar o Log.FileAdapter para a função Q3Reporter.parse/2:

test "should return game contents with valid file and default mode" do assert {:ok, results} = Q3Reporter.parse(@path, mode: :by_game, log_adapter: FileAdapter) assert %Results{entries: [%{}], mode: :by_game} = results
end

Cobertura de Testes

Enfrentei outro problema ao tentar testar erros relacionados a File.read/1, como "falta de memória" e "permissões". Porém não é viável eu manipular arquivos os quais eu não tenho permissões no projeto, e nem lotar a memória do meu computador enquanto os testes eram realizados.

A minha primeira solução, no caso, foi ignorar esses trechos de código na cobertura de testes:

def parse(path, opts \\ []) do with {:ok, content} <- Log.read(path, opts[:log_adapter]), results <- Core.log_to_results(content, opts[:mode]) do {:ok, results} else {:error, :enoent} -> {:error, "'#{path}' not found..."} # coveralls-ignore-start {:error, :eacces} -> {:error, "You don't have permission to open '#{path}..."} {:error, :enomem} -> {:error, "There's no enough memory to open 'invalid'..."} {:error, _} -> {:error, "Error trying to open 'invalid'"} # coveralls-ignore-stop end end

Como a refatoração da sessão anterior me permitiu substituir o adapter, logo eu poderia testar esse trecho da seguinte maneira:

test "should return error with invalid file path" do assert {:error, "'invalid' not found..."} = Q3Reporter.parse("invalid", log_adapter: FileAdapter) assert {:error, "You don't have permission to open 'invalid'..."} = Q3Reporter.parse("invalid", log_adapter: error_adapter(:eacces)) assert {:error, "There's no enough memory to open 'invalid'..."} = Q3Reporter.parse("invalid", log_adapter: error_adapter(:enomem)) assert {:error, "Error trying to open 'invalid'"} = Q3Reporter.parse("invalid", log_adapter: error_adapter(:unknown))
end

O error_adapter/1 não é nada mais do que uma função que utiliza o Mox para mockar um adapter, e retornar o erro esperado:

def error_adapter(error) do module = module_name(error) Mox.defmock(module, for: Q3Reporter.Log) module |> expect(:read, fn _ -> {:error, error} end) |> expect(:mtime, fn _ -> {:error, error} end)
end

Logo, substituir o File por um contrato explícito através de Log me permitiu atingir uma cobertura de testes onde não era possível anteriormente, e eu pude tirar todos os #coveralls-ignore do projeto e ter 100% de cobertura de testes real.

É possível conferir o código de exemplo desse projeto em: https://github.com/maxmaccari/q3_reporter

Mangabo Kolawole - Apr 17

Diego (Relatable Code) - Apr 25
Items.Author: maxmaccari

Items.Title: 将内容回显到文件 | echo content to file
Items.Description:
您可以使用echo命令将一些内容放入文件中，但默认情况下，它会在文件中添加一个尾随“换行符”。 因此，如果您要计算文件的指纹，则需要知道它计算了换行符。

例如，如果您将 "hello" 回显到文件然后计算 md5，则该值将不同于直接计算md5("hello") 。

如果你不想 echo 添加这个换行符，你可以使用-n选项：

 $ man echo -n , Do not print the trailing newline character

例子：

 $ echo "hello" > a1.txt $ md5 a1.txt MD5 (a1.txt) = b1946ac92492d2347c6235b4d2611184 $ cat a1.txt hello $ echo -n "hello" > a2.txt $ md5 a2.txt MD5 (a2.txt) = 5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592 $ cat a2.txt hello%

你可以看到当使用echo -n然后使用cat显示文件内容时，它会显示一个额外的“%”，表示没有“换行符”。 您还可以看到，使用和不使用-n选项时，md5 值是不同的。



原文:

You can use the echo command to put some content into a file, but by default it will add a trailing "newline character" to the file. So if you are calculate file's fingerprint, you need to know it calculated the newline character.

For example, if you echo "hello" to a file then calculate the md5, the value will be different from direct calculate md5("hello").

If you don't want echo to add this newline character, you could use the -n option:

$ man echo
-n,  Do not print the trailing newline character

Example:

$ echo "hello" > a1.txt
$ md5 a1.txt
MD5 (a1.txt) = b1946ac92492d2347c6235b4d2611184
$ cat a1.txt
hello
$ echo -n "hello" > a2.txt
$ md5 a2.txt
MD5 (a2.txt) = 5d41402abc4b2a76b9719d911017c592
$ cat a2.txt
hello%

You can see when use echo -n then use cat to show the file content, it will display an extra "%" to indicate that there is no "newline character". Also you can see, with and without the -n option, the md5 value are different.

Items.Description: 原文:
Items.Author: tookooltech

Items.Title: 使用 Puma-dev 时在 Visual Studio Code 中调试 Rails | Debugging Rails in Visual Studio Code When Using Puma-dev
Items.Description:
来自 Swift 和 Objective-C 等语言，使用适当的调试器，我不必编辑代码来记录某些内容或暂停执行，这对于快速跟踪问题非常关键。 所以——我想——我们在开发过程中使用puma-dev来运行我们的 Rails 应用程序，这是一种常见的设置，因此必须有简单的说明来说明如何将 Visual Studio Code 的调试器附加到正在运行的应用程序。 好吧，不是我能找到的。 通过阅读 Stack Overflow 帖子和实验，我确实设法将它拼凑在一起。

所以，就在这里。

设置调试端口环境变量

首先，我们需要在 puma-dev 加载的地方添加一个环境变量。 我把我的放在~/.powconfig中，因为这是第一个被加载的东西，它在我的主目录中。

编辑~/.powconfig以包含以下内容：

export RUBY_DEBUG_PORT=1234

更新 Gemfile

确保你在:development下有这些：

 gem "ruby-debug-ide" gem "debase" # or byebug

我确信有一种方法可以使用与 Ruby 3.1+ ( rdbg ) 一起打包的新调试器来完成这项工作，但这将是另一次练习。

添加 Rails 初始化程序以启动rdebug-ide

在/config/initializers/中创建一个名为start_debugger.rb的文件（或任何您想要的文件），并添加以下代码：

 if Rails . env . development? && ENV [ 'RUBY_DEBUG_PORT' ] require "ruby-debug-ide" Debugger . start_server nil , ENV [ 'RUBY_DEBUG_PORT' ]. to_i end

在 Visual Studio Code 中将启动目标添加到项目

将此配置添加到<app root>/.vscode/launch.json ：

 { "configurations" : [ { "name" : "Remote Debug" , "type" : "Ruby" , "request" : "attach" , "remoteHost" : "127.0.0.1" , "remotePort" : "1234" , "remoteWorkspaceRoot" : "${workspaceRoot}" , "cwd" : "${workspaceRoot}" , } ]
}

最后

使用touch tmp/restart.txt重新启动您的应用程序。

通过运行“远程调试”、在其中一个控制器中设置断点并刷新将触发代码的页面来对其进行测试。 如果一切设置正确，您的应用程序应该在断点处暂停。

恭喜！ 🎉 🎉



原文:

Coming from languages like Swift and Objective-C, using a proper debugger where I don’t have to edit my code to log something or pause execution is pretty critical for tracking down issues quickly. So — I thought — we’re using puma-dev to run our Rails app during development, that’s a common setup, and so there must be easy instructions for how to attach Visual Studio Code’s debugger to a running app. Well, not that I could find. I did manage to patch it together through a combination of reading Stack Overflow posts and experimentation.

So, here it is.

Set up debug port environment variable

First, we need to add an environment variable in a place that puma-dev will load from. I put mine in ~/.powconfig, because that’s the first thing that gets loaded and it’s in my home directory.

Edit ~/.powconfig to include this:

export RUBY_DEBUG_PORT=1234

Update Gemfile

Make sure you’ve got these under :development:

gem "ruby-debug-ide"
gem "debase" # or byebug

I’m sure there’s a way to make this work with the new debugger that comes packaged with Ruby 3.1+ (rdbg), but that’s going to be an exercise for another time.

Add Rails initializer to start rdebug-ide

Create a file in /config/initializers/ called start_debugger.rb (or whatever you want), and add this code:

if Rails.env.development? && ENV['RUBY_DEBUG_PORT'] require "ruby-debug-ide" Debugger.start_server nil, ENV['RUBY_DEBUG_PORT'].to_i
end

Add Launch Target to project in Visual Studio Code

Add this configuration to <app root>/.vscode/launch.json:

{ "configurations": [ { "name": "Remote Debug", "type": "Ruby", "request": "attach", "remoteHost": "127.0.0.1", "remotePort": "1234", "remoteWorkspaceRoot": "${workspaceRoot}", "cwd": "${workspaceRoot}", } ]
}

Finally

Restart your app with touch tmp/restart.txt.

Test it out by running “Remote Debug”, setting a breakpoint in one of your controllers, and refreshing the page that will trigger the code. If everything is setup correctly, your app should pause at the breakpoint.

Congratulations! 🎉
Items.Author: collindonnell

Items.Title: 2 Hal Penting ! 2 哈尔彭廷！ (akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan) (akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan) | 2 Hal Penting ! (akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan)
Items.Description:
散文 sebuah akuntansi itu pada perhitungan atau hitung-hitungan uang。 目标 pasar menjadi senjata andalan sebuah perusahaan untuk mendistribusikan barang yang akan di jual。 沉迷：mirzacasper.blogspot.com



原文:

Pengetahuan Akuntansi Bagi Masyarakat Saat Ini

Sudah tak asing lagi saat kita mendengar akuntansi saat ini. Akuntansi sangat populer apalagi bagi para kalangan mahasiswa ekonomi di setiap universitas yang ada. Jikalau kita dalami perihal tentang akuntansi pasti berkaitan dengan 2 hal, yaitu : akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan.

Akuntansi manajemen merupakan kegiatan atau proses yang menghasilkan informasi dalam bentuk keuangan bagi manajemen untuk pengambilan sebuah keputusan ekonomi dalam menjalankan fungsi manajemen menurut Halim dan Supomo (2000 : 3). Sedangkan akuntansi keuangan adalah bidang akuntansi yang berkaitan dengan pencatatan & pelaporan data ekonomi suatu perusahaan dan laporan tersebut akan menghasilkan berbagai informasi keuangan yang berguna bagi pemerintah, pemilik perusahaan, kreditur, dan masyarakat dalam pengambilan keputusan menurut Niswonger, Fess dan Warrant ( 1999 : 10).

Akuntansi keuangan dan akuntansi manajemen mempunyai dua hal yang berbeda yaitu objek yang dikaji pada bidang ilmu tersebut. adapun akuntansi manajemen membahas tentang informasi cara mengatur tentang keuangan sebuah perusahaan untuk mengambil sebuah keputusan. Berbagai macam cara untuk dapat mengatur keuangan perusahaan agar dapat menghasilkan keuntungan yang sebanyak- banyaknya bagi perusahaan. Begitu pula dengan akuntansi keuangan yang mencatat dan melaporkan data keuangan perusahaan untuk meminimalkan pengeluaran pada neraca keuangan perusahaan tersebut. Akuntansi keuangan dan akuntansi manajemen mempunyai objek kajian yang berbeda dan manfaat yang berbeda pula .

Akuntansi manajemen lebih mengarah pada informasi untuk menjalankan fungsi manajemen keluar masuk nya keuangan perusahaan atau siklus keuangan perusahaan. Sedangankan akuntansi keuangan mengarah pada pelaporan dan pencatatan penggunaan keuangan pada perusahaan agar dapat menghasilkan keuntungan sebanyak-banyaknya dan kerugian sekecil-kecilnya. Proses sebuah akuntansi itu pada perhitungan atau hitung-hitungan uang. Jadi akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan sama-sama tentang hitung-hitung tapi mempunyai objek kajian yang berbeda.

Kebanyakan orang awam hanya mengira akuntansi hanya sekedar menghitung hitung uang saja akan tetapi akuntansi dapat digunakan untuk menghancurkan sebuah Negara menurut Dr. Irsyadillah, S.pd, M.pd. (dosen fkip Ekonomi Universitas Syiah Kuala). Negara miskin, negara yang krisis rentan korupsi, negara yang krisis ekonomi, dan permasalah lainnya itu di sebabkan kurangnya akuntansi ataupun perhitungan yang salah pada negara tersebut. Itulah penyebab pentingnya kita untuk mempelajari akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan.

Setiap individu membutuhkan akuntansi untuk dapat di praktekkan dalam kehidupan sehari-hari. Misalnya saat mengitung uang belanja setiap bulannya, memberi uang jajan pada si anak, mengatur keuangan setiap bulannya dan itu semua adalah salah satu permasalahan yang di hadapi oleh setiap orang tua. Tidak hanya orang tua yang mengalami permasalahan tersebut, anak-anak dan remaja juga mengalami hal yang serupa juga.

Semua permasalahan itu dapat di selesaikan hanya dengan pengetahuan akuntansi. Akuntansi tanpa kita sadari mempunyai peran yang sangat besar untuk setiap permasalahan yang kita hadapi saat ini dan hingga saat ini pula tidak banyak orang yang menyadari bahwa kita telah menggunakan akuntansi dalam kehidupan sehari-hari nya. Saat menghitung uang, saat menabung, saat memprediksi keuangan, saat membeli, saat membayar dan juga kegaiatan lainnya yang berhubungan dengan hitung-menghitung uang itu termasuk ke dalam akuntansi. Akuntansi tersebut cuma sekedar akuntansi umum yang di praktekkan oleh masyarakat khalayak ramai.

Realita saat ini yang sudah sangat kacau, mengharuskan tiap-tiap individu masyarakat untuk mempelajari akuntansi baik akuntansi manajemen maupun akuntansi keuangan. Korupsi dimana-mana, harga barang semakin naik, inflasi sudah menjadi hal yang biasa, mata uang menurun, stabilitas ekonomi kacau, kalah persaingan dengan negara asing, maraknya eksploitasi asing terhadap sumber daya alam indonesia yang berlimpah, hal-hal tersebut menyebabkan negara tercinta kita ini di injak- injak oleh negara lain karena bobroknya stabilitas negara ini. Keadaan ini di sebabkan oleh lemahnya pengetahuan masyarakat kita terhadap pengetahuan akuntansi. Hal inilah yang meyebabkan pentingnya pengetahuan akuntansi agar dapat memperbaiki dan menstabilkan keuangan keluarga, masyarakat dan juga negara republik indonesia kita tercinta ini.

Masyarakat yang kelaparan dimana- mana, kemiskinan merajalela, kriminalitas menjadi hal yang biasa, dan itu semua siapa sih yang bertanggung jawab atas hal tersebut ?, so pasti pemerintah kita saat ini. Pemerintah sebagai agent dari masyarakat yaitu sebagai orang yang kita percayakan untuk mengurus negara ini harus mengambil sikap dan keputusan untuk mengemban amanah yang telah di berikan rakyat indonesia saat ini. Peran pemerintah terhadap permasalahan yang kita hadapi saat ini sangat dibutuhkan untuk menyelesaikan permasalah yang kita hadapi saat ini.

Permasalahan – permasalahan tersebut menjadi tolak ukur bagi kita para pemuda saat ini bahwa ilmu pengetahuan itu sangat penting. Apalagi pengetahuan tentang akuntansi keuangan dan akuntansi manajemen yang menjadi dasar bagi kita untuk mengelola keuangan yang menjadi akar dari permasalahan yang kita hadapi saat ini. Saat ini uang sudah seperti sesuatu yang paling berharga di karenakan uang sudah menjadi patokan tiap – tiap individu untuk mencapai segala keinginannya bahkan hukum yang telah tertulis pada undang – undang dapat di beli hanya dengan kegilaan manusia terhadap uang. Bagaimana jika negara kita sendiri mencetak uang sebanyak banyaknya saja ?

Segala sesuatu yang tidak mungkin akan menjadi mungkin dengan uang seperti maraknya para agent kita yaitu para dewan yang telah di percayakan oleh masyarakat untuk mengemban amanah untuk mengurus segala permasalahan negara ini, malah mereka yang melakukan hal yang tidak terpuji yaitu seperti para anggota DPR, para pemimpin daerah seperi bupati, gubernur dan lain-lain (OKNUM). Tergila – gila nya manusia terhadap uang di sebabkan kurangnya moral etika, dan pengetahuan tentang agama. Semakin tingginya ilmu seseorang bagaikan “padi yang semakin merunduk” yang bermakna bahwa semakin tingginya ilmu seseorang maka semakin rendah hati orang tersebut, selalu merasa cukup dan tidak mengambil yang bukan hak miliknya.

Tingginya jabatan dan tingginya pangkat seseorang menyebabkan semakin tinggi pula ia di terpa angin oleh berbagai masalah yang akan di hadapi oleh orang tersebut. Tingginya jabatan seseorang belum tentu ilmu yang dimiliki orang tersebut tinggi pula, akan tetapi orang yang berilmu sudah tentu tinggi derajatnnya. Begitu juga dengan akuntansi, semakin tinggi ilmu akuntansi yang dimiliki oleh orang tersebut maka semakin bagus pula ia untuk mengatur dan mengelola keuangannya, karena percaya atau tidak percaya kita harus menyadar bahwa pendidikan finansial itu sangat penting. Akuntansi selain berkaitan dengan aktivitas sehari-hari, akuntansi juga berkaitan dengan bisnis, pendidikan, ekonomi, perusahaan, perkantoran dan lain-lain.

Jikalau kita berbicara tentang bisnis, akuntansi menjadi alat yang vital bagi seorang pebisnis. Maju mundurnya bisnis seseorang tersebut terletak pada akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan pada perusahaan tersebut. Seorang akuntan yang handal akan selalu mampu untuk mengetahui kondisi keuangan sebuah perusahaan dan mampu untuk mengkondisikan dengan situasi ekonomi yang terjadi saat ini. Situasi ekonomi negeri kita tercinta ini sedang di terpa angin badai, hutang luar negeri semakin menumpuk, pengangguran merajalela, kemiskinan di seluruh pelosok daerah, banyak anak terkena gizi buruk, merebaknya virus corona, hiv / aids, dan masih banyak lagi virus yang menimpa negeri ini. seorang akuntan harus mampu melihat kondisi ekonomi untuk mencari peluang agar dapat menghasilkan keuntungan sebanyak- banyaknya dan meminimalkan kerugian sekecil-kecilnya.

Pendidikan juga berkaitan dengan akuntansi, saat seorang guru mengajar di butuhkan perhitungan agar dapat menjawab segala kesulitan yang di hadapi oleh si anak murid dan dari situlah guru berperan untuk menuntun anak tersebut dalam proses pembelajaran. sehingga kita mengerti cara memahami peserta didik dengan baik dan benar. Akuntansi juga berkaitan dengan perusahaan untuk kemajuan dalam memproduksi barang yang akan di jual. Target pasar menjadi senjata andalan sebuah perusahaan untuk mendistribusikan barang yang akan di jual. Di situlah peran akuntansi dalam memperhitungkan atau memprediksikan barang – barang yang cocok dan sesuai dengan objek pasar yang akan di tuju. Andaikan saja perusahaan tersebut hanya mendistribusikan barangnya tanpa memperhitungkan atau memprediksikan, maka kemungkinan besar barang tersebut tidak akan terjual dengan maksimal.

Ekonomi menjadi tolak ukur sebuah negara itu maju. Ekonomi tidak boleh lepas dari akuntansi. Cara mengelola pendapatan sebuah negara menggunakan perhitungan akuntansi seperti pajak, APBN, APBA, APBD, fasilitas negara, perkantoran, BUMN, BUMS, ASURANSI dan berbagai macam bentuk pemdapatan negara yang lain. Semua itu tidak terlepas dari akuntansi. Realita saat ini menunjukkan bahwa negara kita ini sedang dalam mimpi dan angan-angan yang tinggi untuk maju, akan tetapi implementasinya ataupun praktek nya masih kurang hingga pajak saja mampu di lahap semua oleh si “GAYUS” itu, pabrik pesawat habibi yang menjadi salah satu pelopor kemajuan indonesia kini mati total, kapan majunya negara kita ini ??? kesadaran akan ilmu itu sangat penting khususnya akuntansi.

Negara kita memiliki sumber daya alam yang melimpah ruah, SDM yang handal, akan tetapi SDM negara kita banyak yang bekerja di luar negeri seperti “HABIBI” begitu juga dengan SDA yang kita miliki melimpah ruah hingga para negara asing dapat mengekploitasi SDA milik negara kita yang tercinta ini. Kurangnya ilmu dan pengetahuan menyebabkan kita mudah di bodohi oleh bangsa lain. SDA yang menjadi hak milik kita malah di kuasai oleh pihak asing seperti freeport yang berada di papua. Apa daya bangsa ini yang haus akan kemajuan malah terperosok ke dalam jurang kebodohan yang kita rasakan saat ini. Di sini lah peran pemerintah untuk menunjukkan jati diri sebagai penguasa negeri ini untuk mengubah negeri ini menjadi lebih baik.

Mengantisipasi kebodohan adalah cara yang paling ampuh untuk memajukan negeri ini melalui pendidikan, pelatihan, penyuluhan dan berbagai kegiatan yang dapat membangun kreativitas masyarakat indonesia saat ini. Oleh karena itu, peran pemerintah menjadi ujung tombak untuk kemajuan negara kita saat ini. Fakta ini menjadi bukti bahwa pentingnya pengetahuan tentang ilmu akuntansi (akuntansi manajemen dan akuntansi keuangan ).

Penulis : Ridho Fatwa, S.Pd., M.Si. (Pengajar di Nurul Fikri Boarding School Aceh)

sumber : mirzacasper.blogspot.com
